api/AshesOfVelsingrad.AI.AIAction.html.title: Enum AIAction | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.AI.AIAction.html.summary: Enum AIAction Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.AI Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Represents the types of actions an AI can take. public enum AIAction Fields Move = 1 Move to a new position only. MoveAndSkill = 0 Move to a new position then use a skill. Pass = 3 Do nothing and end turn. UseSkill = 2 Use a skill or ability without moving.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.AI.AIPersonality.h.title: Enum AIPersonality | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.AI.AIPersonality.h.summary: Enum AIPersonality Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.AI Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Defines different AI personality types that determine decision-making behavior. public enum AIPersonality Fields Aggressive = 0 Attacks the nearest target, prefers close combat. Balanced = 3 Balances offense and positioning, adapts to situation. Defensive = 2 Maintains distance, avoids direct combat when possible. Opportunistic = 1 Targets weakest enemies, tries to finish off low HP units.

Fields _unit public UnitSystem? _unit Field Value UnitSystem Properties EnableDebugVisualization [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public bool EnableDebugVisualization { get; set; } Property Value bool ThinkingDelayMax [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float ThinkingDelayMax { get; set; } Property Value float ThinkingDelayMin [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float ThinkingDelayMin { get; set; } Property Value float Unit Read-only access to the UnitSystem this AI is attached to. Useful for external systems that need to query the AI's unit. public UnitSystem? Unit { get; } Property Value UnitSystem Methods DecideTurn(BattleState) Returns the AI's decision without executing it. Execution is handled by EnemyAIManager. public virtual Task<AIDecision> DecideTurn(BattleState battleState) Parameters battleState BattleState Returns Task<AIDecision> _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

The unit is AI-driven — attach an EnemyAIBehavior child node in the scene to give it tactical decisions. Methods Initialize() Initializes the unit instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the unit's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioBus.htm.title: Enum AudioBus | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioBus.htm.summary: Enum AudioBus Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Logical mixing channels exposed by the audio service. public enum AudioBus Fields Ambient = 3 Looping ambient layers (wind, crickets, dungeon hum). Master = 0 Master output. Scales every other bus. Music = 1 Background music (single track at a time, crossfaded). Sfx = 2 One-shot sound effects (footsteps, hits, spell casts). Ui = 4 UI clicks, menu transitions, notifications. Voice = 5 Spoken dialogue / VO lines. Remarks The values double as indices into AudioServer bus tables on the Godot side. The Core layer never resolves them itself — it only routes intent ("play this on the music bus") so the adapter can map each AudioBus to a real bus and a settings key. Adding a new bus is a deliberate design choice: every bus needs a settings entry (audio.volume.<name>) and a Godot bus of the same name. Keep the list small.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioCatalog.title: Class AudioCatalog | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioCatalog.summary: Class AudioCatalog Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Single source of truth for every audio track shipped with the game. public static class AudioCatalog Inheritance object AudioCatalog Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Lives in Core (no Godot dependency) so adding a new track is a one-file change that's covered by unit tests. The static constants below are the public ids — call sites use them instead of hard-coded strings. To add a new track, declare a public const string identifier and add a matching registry.Register(...) call inside RegisterDefaults(IAudioRegistry). Keep the id namespaced (music.foo, sfx.bar) so they sort sensibly. Fields BattleTheme Battle theme. Selected by Battle in AudioManager.SetMusicContext. Currently bound to the prison encounter track; later battles can override per-encounter or the catalog can grow more music.battle.x ids. public const string BattleTheme = "music.battle" Field Value string ExplorationTheme Exploration / world-map theme. Selected by Exploration in AudioManager.SetMusicContext. Currently bound to the prison-tier track until the open world ships its own. public const string ExplorationTheme = "music.exploration" Field Value string MainMenuTheme Main menu theme. Plays on a loop while the menu is open. public const string MainMenuTheme = "music.main_menu" Field Value string Methods RegisterDefaults(IAudioRegistry) Populates registry with every track shipped with the game. Calling twice on the same registry throws — use Clear() first if a hot reload is intended. public static void RegisterDefaults(IAudioRegistry registry) Parameters registry IAudioRegistry Registry to populate. Exceptions ArgumentNullException When registry is null.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioTrack.h.title: Class AudioTrack | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioTrack.h.summary: Class AudioTrack Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Static metadata describing one playable audio asset. public sealed record AudioTrack : IEquatable<AudioTrack> Inheritance object AudioTrack Implements IEquatable<AudioTrack> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Tracks are registered up-front in AudioCatalog and looked up by Id at play time, so call sites stay decoupled from res:// paths. BaseVolumeMultiplier is a per-track linear multiplier baked into the asset's metadata — useful when one track was mastered louder than the rest. It stacks on top of the bus volume from settings, so 0.5 means "always play this song at half volume relative to its bus". Constructors AudioTrack(string, string, AudioBus, float, bool) Static metadata describing one playable audio asset. public AudioTrack(string Id, string ResourcePath, AudioBus Bus, float BaseVolumeMultiplier = 1, bool Loop = true) Parameters Id string Stable, human-readable identifier (e.g. "music.main_menu"). Must be unique across the registry; reserved as a string constant on AudioCatalog. ResourcePath string Godot resource path (res://...) pointing at the audio asset. Bus AudioBus Logical bus this track plays on. Decides which dispatch path Play(string) takes (music crossfade, ambient layer, SFX/UI/Voice one-shot). BaseVolumeMultiplier float Per-track linear multiplier in [0, 1]. 1.0 = unmodified, 0.5 = -6 dB attenuation on top of the bus. Loop bool Whether the track should loop when it finishes. Music and Ambient default to true; one-shots should set this to false. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioVolumeM.title: Class AudioVolumeMath | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.AudioVolumeM.summary: Class AudioVolumeMath Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Pure-C# math helpers shared by the audio service for converting between the 0..1 linear scale stored in user settings and the decibel scale Godot's AudioServer consumes. public static class AudioVolumeMath Inheritance object AudioVolumeMath Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Lives in the Core assembly so the conversion is unit-tested without a Godot runtime. The adapter calls into these helpers instead of hand-rolling the math in two places. Anything below MinAudibleLinear is treated as full silence and clamped to SilenceDb, which keeps log10(0) from blowing up and matches the intuitive "slider all the way down = muted" behaviour. Fields MaxLinear Upper bound of the linear (settings) volume scale. public const float MaxLinear = 1 Field Value float MinAudibleLinear Linear values at or below this are treated as full silence. public const float MinAudibleLinear = 0.0001 Field Value float MinLinear Lower bound of the linear (settings) volume scale. public const float MinLinear = 0 Field Value float SilenceDb Decibel value used to represent silence on a Godot bus. public const float SilenceDb = -80 Field Value float Methods ClampLinear(float) Clamps an arbitrary float into the supported linear range [MinLinear, MaxLinear]. public static float ClampLinear(float linear) Parameters linear float A linear volume, typically from a UI slider. Returns float The input clamped to the legal range. DbToLinear(float) Converts a decibel value into the 0..1 linear scale used by settings. public static float DbToLinear(float db) Parameters db float Decibel value, typically from AudioServer.GetBusVolumeDb. Returns float Linear volume in [0,1]. Values at or below SilenceDb become MinLinear. LinearToDb(float) Converts a 0..1 linear volume into decibels suitable for AudioServer.SetBusVolumeDb. public static float LinearToDb(float linear) Parameters linear float Linear volume; values outside [0,1] are clamped. Returns float Decibel value, or SilenceDb for inputs at or below MinAudibleLinear.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.IAudioRegist.summary: Interface IAudioRegistry Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Lookup table for every AudioTrack known to the game. public interface IAudioRegistry Remarks The registry is populated at start-up by AudioCatalog and queried by IAudioService when call sites use a track id instead of a raw resource path. Treat ids as the public contract — they live as const fields on AudioCatalog and survive asset-path renames. Implementations are expected to be case-insensitive on ids and to reject duplicate registrations explicitly so a typo in two catalog entries doesn't silently overwrite the first one. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.IAudioServic.title: Interface IAudioService | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.IAudioServic.summary: Interface IAudioService Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Contract for the central audio service. public interface IAudioService Remarks Defined in Core so gameplay code (skills, status effects, exploration triggers) can request audio without taking a hard reference to Godot. The adapter (AudioManager) implements this interface against AudioServer and the Godot scene tree. All track parameters are res:// paths (or any path Godot's ResourceLoader understands). Volume parameters use the 0..1 linear scale; the adapter converts to decibels via AudioVolumeMath. Properties Registry Track registry exposed for read access (UI listings, debug overlays, etc.) and for tests that need to assert what's been registered. IAudioRegistry Registry { get; } Property Value IAudioRegistry Methods GetBusVolume(AudioBus) Returns the current volume for a bus on the 0..1 linear scale. float GetBusVolume(AudioBus bus) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to query. Returns float Linear volume (0 = silent, 1 = unity gain). IsBusMuted(AudioBus) Returns whether a bus is currently muted. bool IsBusMuted(AudioBus bus) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to query. Returns bool Play(string) Plays the registered track with id trackId, dispatching to the right code path (music crossfade, ambient layer, or one-shot) based on the track's Bus. The per-track BaseVolumeMultiplier is applied on top of the bus volume so individual songs that were mastered too loud can be tamed at the catalog level. void Play(string trackId) Parameters trackId string Identifier registered through AudioCatalog. PlayAmbient(string, string, float) Starts (or replaces) an ambient layer. Multiple layers can play at once, each addressed by layerId. void PlayAmbient(string layerId, string trackPath, float fadeSeconds = 1.5) Parameters layerId string Stable identifier for the layer (e.g. "wind", "crickets"). trackPath string Resource path of the ambient stream. fadeSeconds float Fade-in duration. PlayMusic(string, float, bool) Starts (or crossfades into) a music track. void PlayMusic(string trackPath, float fadeSeconds = 1.5, bool loop = true) Parameters trackPath string Resource path of the music stream. fadeSeconds float Crossfade duration with the currently playing track. loop bool Whether the stream should loop when it reaches the end. PlaySfx(string, float, float) Plays a one-shot sound effect through the SFX pool. void PlaySfx(string trackPath, float volumeLinear = 1, float pitchScale = 1) Parameters trackPath string Resource path of the SFX stream. volumeLinear float Per-shot volume scale on the 0..1 linear scale. pitchScale float Pitch multiplier (1.0 = original pitch). PlayUi(string, float) Plays a one-shot UI sound through the UI bus. void PlayUi(string trackPath, float volumeLinear = 1) Parameters trackPath string Resource path of the UI stream. volumeLinear float Per-shot volume scale on the 0..1 linear scale. SetBusMuted(AudioBus, bool) Mutes or unmutes a bus. void SetBusMuted(AudioBus bus, bool muted) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to toggle. muted bool true to mute, false to restore the persisted volume. SetBusVolume(AudioBus, float) Sets the volume for a bus and persists it through the settings layer. void SetBusVolume(AudioBus bus, float linear) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to set. linear float New linear volume; values outside [0,1] are clamped. StopAllAmbient(float) Fades out and stops every active ambient layer. void StopAllAmbient(float fadeSeconds = 1) Parameters fadeSeconds float Fade-out duration applied to each layer. StopAmbient(string, float) Fades out and stops a single ambient layer. void StopAmbient(string layerId, float fadeSeconds = 1) Parameters layerId string Identifier passed to PlayAmbient(string, string, float). fadeSeconds float Fade-out duration. StopMusic(float) Fades out and stops the current music track, if any. void StopMusic(float fadeSeconds = 1) Parameters fadeSeconds float Fade-out duration. 0 stops immediately.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.MusicContext.title: Enum MusicContext | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio.MusicContext.summary: Enum MusicContext Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Audio Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll High-level scene categories used to pick the right music track. public enum MusicContext Fields Battle = 3 In-battle — plays BattleTheme when registered. Exploration = 2 Exploration / world map — plays ExplorationTheme when registered. MainMenu = 1 Main menu / title screen / settings — plays MainMenuTheme. None = 0 No declared context. Any music currently playing fades out. Remarks Scenes call AudioManager.Instance?.SetMusicContext(...) on _Ready (or whenever they enter a new gameplay phase) to declare what kind of music should be playing. The manager resolves the context to a AudioCatalog id and either crossfades to that track or — if no track is registered for the context yet — fades the current music out. This is what keeps the main-menu theme from leaking into exploration and battle: the menu scene declares MainMenu, the exploration scene declares Exploration, and the battle scene declares Battle. As long as every gameplay scene sets its own context, the audio layer always knows what should be playing — even before the exploration / battle tracks ship.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Corruption.Co.title: Class CorruptedTransformationEffect | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Corruption.Co.summary: Class CorruptedTransformationEffect Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Corruption Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Time-limited effect that turns its bearer into an uncontrollable monster. public sealed class CorruptedTransformationEffect : StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>, IStatusEffect Inheritance object StatusEffect<IUnitSystem> CorruptedTransformationEffect Implements IStatusEffect Inherited Members StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.Name StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.Description StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.Duration StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.StackCount StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.ModifierType StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.Amount StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.IsStackable StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.IsPurifiable StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.ShouldApplyTwice StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.EffectToSpread StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.OnTurnPassed(IUnitSystem) StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.AddStack() StatusEffect<IUnitSystem>.ResetDuration(int) object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Triggered by reaching corruption level 3, or by the dedicated Conversion Corrompue dark spell with a 3-turn duration. While active: The bearer's Faction is overridden to Enemy. The unit is AI-controlled and attacks the nearest target regardless of its original allegiance. When the effect expires, OnTransformationEnd(IUnitSystem, Faction) restores OriginalFaction. The unit's persistent corruption tier stays at the level that caused the transformation. Constructors CorruptedTransformationEffect(Faction, int) Build a new transformation effect. public CorruptedTransformationEffect(Faction originalFaction, int duration = 3) Parameters originalFaction Faction Faction to restore when the effect expires. duration int Number of turns the unit stays transformed. Properties OriginalFaction The faction the unit had before transformation, restored on expiration. public Faction OriginalFaction { get; } Property Value Faction Methods OnApply(IUnitSystem) Called when this effect is applied to a target. Override this to implement custom logic (e.g., visual/audio feedback, stat changes). public override void OnApply(IUnitSystem target) Parameters target IUnitSystem The target receiving the effect. OnRemove(IUnitSystem) Called when this effect is removed from a target. Override this to implement cleanup logic (e.g., removing buffs, stopping visual/audio feedback). public override void OnRemove(IUnitSystem target) Parameters target IUnitSystem The target losing the effect.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.EntityProfile.title: Class EntityProfile | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.EntityProfile.summary: Class EntityProfile Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Data Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Designer-authored display metadata attached to a combatant. public sealed record EntityProfile : IEquatable<EntityProfile> Inheritance object EntityProfile Implements IEquatable<EntityProfile> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Pure C# data — name, portrait path, class, level, flavour text — used by HUD widgets (PlayerStatusPanel, TurnOrderQueue, VictoryScreen) to render unit identity. Does NOT carry gameplay state; that lives on IUnitSystem. Modelled as a record class: same reference semantics as a regular class (so a unit and its HUD widget share the same instance and a mutation propagates), but with auto-generated Equals / GetHashCode based on the property values, plus a with-expression for clean profile derivation. Reference semantics are deliberate — a struct would copy on every property access and silently break unit.EntityProfile.Level = 5. The portrait is stored as a res:// path string rather than a Godot.Texture2D: this keeps AshesofVelsingrad.Core Godot-free and unit-testable. The Godot adapter (HUD widgets, in-world sprites) calls ResourceLoader.Load<Texture2D>(profile.PortraitPath) at render time to materialise the texture. File lives in Core/Data/ (matching the namespace) rather than Core/Systems/ — it carries no system behaviour, only data. Properties Bio Optional flavour text for hover tooltips. public string Bio { get; set; } Property Value string ClassName Class label ("Fighter", "Fire Mage", ...) for tooltips and roster screens. public string ClassName { get; set; } Property Value string DisplayName Name shown in HUD widgets and dialogue prompts. public string DisplayName { get; set; } Property Value string Level Level shown next to the name. public int Level { get; set; } Property Value int PortraitPath Godot resource path to the portrait texture (e.g. res://assets/portraits/Pikachu.png). The Godot adapter loads it lazily — Core stays free of Godot.Texture2D so this class is testable without a Godot runtime. Empty string means no portrait; HUD widgets fall back to a coloured placeholder. public string PortraitPath { get; set; } Property Value string

Methods Tr(string) Look up a translated string. Currently a passthrough. public static string Tr(string key) Parameters key string Either an English fallback or a translation key. Returns string The translated string, or key when no translation exists.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Skills.html.title: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Skills | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Skills.html.summary: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Data.Skills Classes ArcaneFocus ArmyOfTheDead Armée des Morts — Army of the Dead. Temporarily summons corpses (placeholder). BladeDance Danse des Lames — Dance of Blades. Strikes up to 3 adjacent enemies. Block Block — Block. Apply a 50% damage-reduction shield to self for 1 turn. BloodStrike Frappe Sanguine — Blood Strike. Damages and heals the caster for 20% of damage dealt. BruteForce Force Brute — +10% melee damage. Apply at battle start; not invoked. Charge Charge — linear movement + attack. The target MUST be on the same row or column as the caster (no diagonals). The caster slides along that axis and stops on the tile immediately before the target, then strikes for 120% ATK. CircularStrike Frappe Circulaire — Circular Strike. Hits every adjacent enemy. CorruptedConversion Conversion Corrompue — the hallmark Darkness-school spell. CrashingWave Vague Déferlante — Crashing Wave. Knocks enemies back, extinguishes fires. CriticalStrike Coup Critique — Critical Strike. Double damage. CD 3 (doc-fixed). CrushingStrike Frappe Écrasante — Crushing Strike. +50% damage; can stun the target. CursedOrb Orbe Maudit — Cursed Orb. Weakens the target. DarkPact Pacte Sombre — Dark Pact. Sacrifice HP to boost magic. DeadlyToxins DivineJudgment Jugement Divin — Divine Judgment. Lightning strike, +200% damage vs corrupted. DivinePrayer Prière Divine — Divine Prayer. +Status resistance for 3 turns. DragonsBreath Souffle du Dragon — Dragon's Breath. Wide cone of fire. ElementalAttunement Execution Exécution — Execute. Instantly kills an enemy below 15% HP. ExecutionerBlade Lame d'Exécution — Execution Blade. +50% damage to targets below 30% HP. Fireball Boule de Feu — Fireball. Damage + burn for 3 turns. Flamestorm Tempête Ardente — Burning Storm. 3×3 area damage that ignites the terrain. FlashFreeze Glaciation Instantanée — Instant Glaciation. 50% chance to stun in a small area. FrostArrow Flèche de Givre — Frost Arrow. Stuns the target for 1 turn. GallantStrike Coup de Bravoure — Brave Strike. Self-buff: -50% damage taken + counter for 1 turn. GraniteSkin Peau de Granit — Granite Skin. +DEF to ally for 3 turns. HawkEye Œil de Faucon — Hawk's Eye. +2 range for 1 turn (modelled as ATK buff stand-in). HealingRain Pluie Guérisseuse — Healing Rain. Light heal on every ally. L10n Tiny localisation indirection so future i18n is a 1-line swap per call site. LethalAim LightningStrike Frappe Éclair — Lightning Strike. Rush + +20% damage at range 5. ManaWell MobileShot MultiShot Pluie de Flèches — Arrow Rain. AoE damage in a 3×3 zone. PhantomStrike Frappe Fantôme — Phantom Strike. Reach attack at 2 tiles distance. PiercingShot Flèche Perforante — Piercing Arrow. Pierces up to 2 aligned enemies. PoisonArrow Flèche Empoisonnée — Poison Arrow. Burning DOT 3 turns (poison stand-in). PreciseShot ProtectiveBubble Bulle de Protection — Protection Bubble. Shield an ally for 1 turn. PurifyingFlash Éclat Purificateur — Purifying Burst. Removes every status effect from one ally. PyromanticBurst Explosion Pyromantique — Pyromantic Explosion. Consumes ALL mana for devastating damage. Recklessness Témérité — +5% damage per 25% missing HP. Wired in damage formula, not as a Use(). Resurrection Résurrection — Resurrection. Revives a fallen ally with 50% HP. Riposte Riposte — 30% chance to counterattack after a melee hit. SacredRay Rayon Sacré — Sacred Ray. Damages enemies, heals allies in its line. SeismicWave Onde Sismique — Seismic Wave. Knocks enemies down in a line; 70% Stun chance. ShadowChains Lame d'Ombre — Shadow Blade. Ignores defense, drains life. ShadowStrike Coup d'Ombre — Shadow Strike. Attacks without breaking invisibility. SkillStrings Single source of truth for every skill's display name and description string. SneakStrike SpectralBlade Lame Spectrale — ignores 15% of enemy armor. StealthyShadow StoneFortress Forteresse de Pierre — Stone Fortress. Wall blocking movement (placeholder). StoneSpike Lance de Roc — Rock Spear. Pierces armor. TaintedBlood Sang Corrompu — Corrupted Blood. Reduces regen and healing on target. TitanAvatar Avatar du Titan — Titan Avatar. Temporary stone-colossus form. Vanish Vanish — Vanish. Become invisible until end of next turn. WallOfFlames Mur de Flammes — Wall of Flames. Barrier; placeholder until terrain effects land. WarCry Cri de Guerre — War Cry. +15% Atk/Def to nearby allies for 3 turns. WarriorEndurance Endurance Guerrière — -15% damage taken after a successful melee strike. WaterJet Jet d'Eau — Water Jet. Moderate damage + slow. WayOfTheBlade Danse du Lame (passive) — +10% crit chance if the unit moved before attacking.

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Default loadout is the Fighter catalogue's basic actives so each soldier already feels like a competent threat in the test scene; if a designer wants a softer or weirder enemy they can subclass and override Initialize(). Stats use the same shape as KaelenVoss at level 1 but slightly weaker so a single soldier is a fair fight for one player. Scale up for later encounters by exporting SoldierLevel and a stat multiplier later. Properties SoldierBaseAtk Base attack. Default sized to be a fair fight for a level-1 fighter. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float SoldierBaseAtk { get; set; } Property Value float SoldierBaseDef Base defence. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float SoldierBaseDef { get; set; } Property Value float SoldierBaseSpeed Base speed — drives turn order. Lower than Kaelen's 180 so the player acts first. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float SoldierBaseSpeed { get; set; } Property Value float SoldierLevel Level shown next to the name in the turn-queue chip / status panel. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public int SoldierLevel { get; set; } Property Value int SoldierMaxHp Max HP. Adjust per-instance for tougher / weaker variants. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float SoldierMaxHp { get; set; } Property Value float SoldierName Display name shown in the HUD. Overridable per-instance. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public string SoldierName { get; set; } Property Value string SoldierPersonality Tactical-AI personality: Aggressive / Defensive / Opportunistic / Balanced. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public AIPersonality SoldierPersonality { get; set; } Property Value AIPersonality SoldierPortraitPath Portrait res:// path. Falls back to a coloured square if empty. [Export(PropertyHint.File, "*.png,*.jpg,*.svg")] public string SoldierPortraitPath { get; set; } Property Value string Methods Initialize() Initializes the unit instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the unit's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic.

TestMenuManager() public TestMenuManager() Properties Instance public static TestMenuManager? Instance { get; set; } Property Value TestMenuManager LastShownMenu public string? LastShownMenu { get; } Property Value string RegisteredMenus public Dictionary<string, Control> RegisteredMenus { get; } Property Value Dictionary<string, Control> Methods Initialize() Initializes the MenuManager singleton instance. Ensures only one instance exists and sets up the initial state. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It checks for duplicate instances and initializes the menu system. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate. RegisterMenu(string, Control) Registers a menu with the MenuManager. Adds the menu to the internal dictionary and hides it by default. public override void RegisterMenu(string menuName, Control menuControl) Parameters menuName string The unique name of the menu to register. menuControl Control The Control instance representing the menu. Remarks This method checks if the menu is already registered to avoid duplicates. If the menu is already registered, it prints an error message and does not add it again. It also connects back signals for navigation menus, such as OptionsMenu, to handle back navigation. ShowMenu(string, bool) Shows a menu by its name. Hides the current menu and shows the specified menu. public override void ShowMenu(string menuName, bool addToHistory = true) Parameters menuName string The unique name of the menu to show. addToHistory bool Whether to add the current menu to the history stack for back navigation. Remarks This method checks if the specified menu exists in the registered menus. If it does, it hides the current menu and shows the specified menu. If the current menu is not empty, it adds it to the history stack for back navigation. If the specified menu is not found, it prints an error message. VerifyInstanceState() public void VerifyInstanceState()

This method can be overridden in derived classes to implement specific cleanup logic. protected override void Cleanup() Remarks This method is called when the unit is removed from the scene tree. It provides a place for derived classes to implement any necessary cleanup logic, such as disconnecting signals or releasing resources. By default, it does nothing, but derived classes can override it to perform specific cleanup tasks. Initialize() Initializes the unit instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the unit's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.IntegrationTests.S.title: Class FactionMarkerTest | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.IntegrationTests.S.summary: Class FactionMarkerTest Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.IntegrationTests.Systems Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Coverage for FactionMarker — the small floating arrow above each unit that's colour-coded by faction. The marker itself has no gameplay logic, but it's how the player visually identifies whose side a unit is on, so a regression here makes the battlefield unreadable. Tests verify: Bind(Faction) assigns a faction-distinct colour. SetActive(bool) brightens the marker for the active turn. The marker mounts a child Godot.MeshInstance3D and floats above its parent. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.IntegrationTests.U.summary:
Class BattleHudTest Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.IntegrationTests.UI Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Coverage for BattleHud — the programmatic HUD root. [TestSuite(24, "")] [RequireGodotRuntime] public class BattleHudTest Inheritance object BattleHudTest Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Verifies the contract GameManager.EnsureHud relies on:
  • Build() creates every child widget exactly once.
  • Build() is idempotent — calling it twice (or before _Ready fires) doesn't double-spawn.
  • Build() pins Layer = HudLayer and Visible = true.
  • Each child widget's own _built flag is set, so subsequent calls are no-ops.

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Player-controlled by default (faction set to Player by GameManager.LoadUnits based on which container the unit lives in). Active loadout uses the Fighter catalogue: CrushingStrike, WarCry, Charge, Block, CircularStrike. Passives are wired into PassiveSkills for future damage-formula hooks. Methods Initialize() Initializes the unit instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the unit's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic.

Value PackedScene _menuContainer [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] protected Control? _menuContainer Field Value Control _optionsMenuScene [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] protected PackedScene? _optionsMenuScene Field Value PackedScene Methods CreateMenus() Create menu instances - virtual for testing overrides. Default implementation instantiates from PackedScenes. Checks for null references and logs appropriately. Can be overridden in tests to provide mock or test-specific menus. protected virtual (Node mainMenu, Node optionsMenu) CreateMenus() Returns (Node mainMenu, Node optionsMenu) Tuple of (mainMenu, optionsMenu) nodes. InitializeMenus() Initialise the menus and register them with the MenuManager. Ensures that the SettingsManager and MenuManager singletons are available. Uses deferred calls to avoid Godot lifecycle issues. Catches and logs exceptions during initialization. Also checks that the menu container and scenes are valid before proceeding. This method can be overridden in tests to provide mock menus. protected virtual void InitializeMenus() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

Instance { get; } Property Value AudioManager Registry Track registry exposed for read access (UI listings, debug overlays, etc.) and for tests that need to assert what's been registered. public IAudioRegistry Registry { get; } Property Value IAudioRegistry Methods GetBusVolume(AudioBus) Returns the current volume for a bus on the 0..1 linear scale. public float GetBusVolume(AudioBus bus) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to query. Returns float Linear volume (0 = silent, 1 = unity gain). GetSettingsKey(AudioBus) public static string GetSettingsKey(AudioBus bus) Parameters bus AudioBus Returns string Initialize() Initializes the manager instance. This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the manager. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the manager's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. IsBusMuted(AudioBus) Returns whether a bus is currently muted. public bool IsBusMuted(AudioBus bus) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to query. Returns bool Play(string) Plays the registered track with id trackId, dispatching to the right code path (music crossfade, ambient layer, or one-shot) based on the track's Bus. The per-track BaseVolumeMultiplier is applied on top of the bus volume so individual songs that were mastered too loud can be tamed at the catalog level. public void Play(string trackId) Parameters trackId string Identifier registered through AudioCatalog. PlayAmbient(string, string, float) Starts (or replaces) an ambient layer. Multiple layers can play at once, each addressed by layerId. public void PlayAmbient(string layerId, string trackPath, float fadeSeconds = 1.5) Parameters layerId string Stable identifier for the layer (e.g. "wind", "crickets"). trackPath string Resource path of the ambient stream. fadeSeconds float Fade-in duration. PlayBootMusic() Boot-time fallback that picks MainMenu if no scene has declared its own context yet. Marked protected (not private) so the integration-test harness can invoke it via reflection on a TestAudioManager subclass — same access modifier as Initialize() for the same reason. protected void PlayBootMusic() PlayMusic(string, float, bool) Starts (or crossfades into) a music track. public void PlayMusic(string trackPath, float fadeSeconds = 1.5, bool loop = true) Parameters trackPath string Resource path of the music stream. fadeSeconds float Crossfade duration with the currently playing track. loop bool Whether the stream should loop when it reaches the end. PlaySfx(string, float, float) Plays a one-shot sound effect through the SFX pool. public void PlaySfx(string trackPath, float volumeLinear = 1, float pitchScale = 1) Parameters trackPath string Resource path of the SFX stream. volumeLinear float Per-shot volume scale on the 0..1 linear scale. pitchScale float Pitch multiplier (1.0 = original pitch). PlayUi(string, float) Plays a one-shot UI sound through the UI bus. public void PlayUi(string trackPath, float volumeLinear = 1) Parameters trackPath string Resource path of the UI stream. volumeLinear float Per-shot volume scale on the 0..1 linear scale. SetBusMuted(AudioBus, bool) Mutes or unmutes a bus. public void SetBusMuted(AudioBus bus, bool muted) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to toggle. muted bool true to mute, false to restore the persisted volume. SetBusVolume(AudioBus, float) Sets the volume for a bus and persists it through the settings layer. public void SetBusVolume(AudioBus bus, float linear) Parameters bus AudioBus Bus to set. linear float New linear volume; values outside [0,1] are clamped. SetMusicContext(MusicContext) Declares what kind of scene is now active so the right music plays. public void SetMusicContext(MusicContext context) Parameters context MusicContext The scene category currently active. Remarks Scenes call this on _Ready (or whenever they enter a new gameplay phase) instead of poking individual track ids — the catalog is then free to re-map a context to a different track without touching every call-site. When the catalog has no track registered for the requested context (typical during development before exploration / battle music ships) the manager fades the current music out, so the menu theme doesn't leak into exploration or combat. Calls are idempotent — passing the current context is a no-op. StopAllAmbient(float) Fades out and stops every active ambient layer. public void StopAllAmbient(float fadeSeconds = 1) Parameters fadeSeconds float Fade-out duration applied to each layer. StopAmbient(string, float) Fades out and stops a single ambient layer. public void StopAmbient(string layerId, float fadeSeconds = 1) Parameters layerId string Identifier passed to PlayAmbient(string, string, float). fadeSeconds float Fade-out duration. StopMusic(float) Fades out and stops the current music track, if any. public void StopMusic(float fadeSeconds = 1) Parameters fadeSeconds float Fade-out duration. 0 stops immediately. _ExitTree() Called when the node is removed from the scene tree. Cleans up the manager instance and sets it to null. public override void _ExitTree() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is removed from the scene tree. It ensures that the manager instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. This is important for preventing memory leaks and ensuring that the manager can be re-initialized later if needed.

class serves as a base for all manager classes, ensuring that only one instance exists. It provides a common initialization and cleanup mechanism for all managers. Derived classes should implement the Initialize method to set up their specific functionality. Properties Instance public static BaseManager? Instance { get; protected set; } Property Value BaseManager Methods Cleanup() Cleans up the manager instance. This method can be overridden in derived classes to implement specific cleanup logic. protected virtual void Cleanup() Remarks This method is called when the manager is removed from the scene tree. It provides a place for derived classes to implement any necessary cleanup logic, such as disconnecting signals or releasing resources. By default, it does nothing, but derived classes can override it to perform specific cleanup tasks. Initialize() Initializes the manager instance. This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected abstract void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the manager. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the manager's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. _ExitTree() Called when the node is removed from the scene tree. Cleans up the manager instance and sets it to null. public override void _ExitTree() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is removed from the scene tree. It ensures that the manager instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. This is important for preventing memory leaks and ensuring that the manager can be re-initialized later if needed. _Ready() Called when the node is added to the scene tree. Initializes the manager instance and checks for duplicates. public override void _Ready() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It ensures that only one instance of the manager exists in the scene tree. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate.

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks The exploration layer (an NpcBattleTrigger on an NPC, an encounter zone, scripted intro fight, …) builds a BattleSetup and calls Launch(BattleSetup). BattleLauncher stores the setup, swaps to the battle scene, and exposes the setup to GameManager via PendingSetup so the GameManager can spawn the configured units. When the battle ends the player can:
  • Try Again — handled inside via ReloadCurrentScene; the launcher's pending setup is preserved.
  • Forfeit — calls here, which loads and queues the player respawn at .
  • Continue (victory) — calls with the same flow.
Register as an autoload (Project Settings → AutoLoad): 
Path: res://scripts/managers/BattleLauncher.cs Name: BattleLauncher Singleton: ✓ If you don't register the autoload,  stays null and exploration code that calls BattleLauncher.Instance?.Launch(...) silently no-ops — Test.tscn keeps working standalone. Properties Instance Singleton instance, set in _Ready(). public static BattleLauncher? Instance { get; } Property Value BattleLauncher PendingSetup The setup currently being launched, or null when no battle is active. GameManager reads this in InitializeGameManager and uses it to populate the unit containers from BattleSetup's PackedScene lists. public BattleSetup? PendingSetup { get; } Property Value BattleSetup Methods ConsumePendingReturnPosition() The player controller in the exploration scene calls this in _Ready to find out whether it should snap to a return position from a finished battle. Returns null when there's no pending return — the player keeps whatever position the exploration scene gave them by default. Consuming clears the stored value so subsequent reads return null. public Vector3? ConsumePendingReturnPosition() Returns Vector3? Forfeit() Player pressed Forfeit on the GameOverScreen. Tear down the battle and return the player to the exploration scene at the saved return position. public void Forfeit() Launch(BattleSetup) Launch a new battle. Stores the setup and swaps to the battle scene. AshesOfVelsingrad.Managers.GameManager.InitializeGameManager() will read PendingSetup to populate the unit containers. public void Launch(BattleSetup setup) Parameters setup BattleSetup Encounter description. VictoryReturn() Player pressed Continue on the VictoryScreen. Same scene transition as forfeit, but callers can layer reward / progression hooks here later. public void VictoryReturn() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Call EnsureHud() + EnsureIndicators() from InitializeGameManager after the map and turn manager are resolved. Then WireHudEvents() connects ActionMenu / SkillSelector buttons to the gameplay handlers. Fields _battleHud HUD root, spawned (or found) in EnsureHud(). protected BattleHud? _battleHud Field Value BattleHud _gameOverScreen End-of-battle Defeat overlay; spawned lazily. protected GameOverScreen? _gameOverScreen Field Value GameOverScreen _indicators World-space tile overlays (move/target/hover). protected IndicatorOverlay? _indicators Field Value IndicatorOverlay _victoryScreen End-of-battle Victory overlay; spawned lazily. protected VictoryScreen? _victoryScreen Field Value VictoryScreen Properties AIManager public EnemyAIManager? AIManager { get; } Property Value EnemyAIManager EnableThreatMapDebug Toggle threat map visualization for all enemy units. Call this from a debug input or console command. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public bool EnableThreatMapDebug { get; set; } Property Value bool Methods AnimateUnitMove((int X, int Y, int Z)) Move the active unit to cell with an A* path-followed tween animation. protected Task AnimateUnitMove((int X, int Y, int Z) cell) Parameters cell (int, int, int) Destination cell. Returns Task A task that completes once the animation finishes. BindHudRosters() Populate widgets that only need a one-shot bind at battle start (enemy roster). protected void BindHudRosters() Remarks Called via CallDeferred after EnsureHud() so the HUD's child widgets have run their _Ready and the EnemyRoster reference is populated. CancelSkillTargeting() Exit skill-targeting mode and return to movement context. public void CancelSkillTargeting() EnsureHud() Find an existing BattleHud in the scene or spawn one. protected void EnsureHud() Remarks Widget internals are only built when the BattleHud's _Ready fires next frame, so any code that touches _battleHud.PlayerStatus.Bind(...), etc. must run via CallDeferred (see RefreshHudOnReady()) rather than synchronously. EnsureIndicators() Spawn the move/target/hover indicator overlay parented to the map. protected void EnsureIndicators() HideAllIndicators() Hide every indicator overlay (move + target + hover). protected void HideAllIndicators() Initialize() Initializes the manager instance. This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the manager. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the manager's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. MoveUnit((int, int, int)) Moves the currently active unit to the specified cell on the map. public virtual void MoveUnit((int, int, int) cell) Parameters cell (int, int, int) The target cell position as a Godot.Vector3I (grid coordinates X, Y, Z). Examples Vector3I targetCell = new Vector3I(2, 0, 3); gameManager.MoveUnit(targetCell); Remarks Checks that both _mapSystemContainer and _turnManagerContainer are properly set before performing the move. If valid, the active unit is repositioned in the world based on the given cell coordinates and the map’s cell size. RefreshHudForActiveUnit(IUnitSystem?) Refresh the player-side HUD bindings (status panel + skill bar) for the active turn. protected void RefreshHudForActiveUnit(IUnitSystem? active) Parameters active IUnitSystem The unit whose turn it is. RefreshHudOnReady() One-shot first-turn HUD refresh — runs AFTER EnsureHud() finishes spawning the BattleHud and its _Ready populates the child widget references. Re-binds the active unit, redraws move tiles and updates the context info panel — all of which silently no-op'd on the very first AshesOfVelsingrad.Managers.GameManager.ActivatePlayerUnit() call because _battleHud's children hadn't been built yet. protected void RefreshHudOnReady() Remarks Scheduled via CallDeferred at the end of InitializeGameManager. ShowGameOverScreen() Spawn the GameOverScreen overlay. Called from CheckWinLoseCondition on defeat. protected void ShowGameOverScreen() ShowMoveIndicators(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)>) Light green tiles for the active unit's reachable cells. protected void ShowMoveIndicators(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)> tiles) Parameters tiles IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)> Move-eligible cells. ShowTargetIndicators(IEnumerable<Vector3I>) Light red tiles where the queued skill can land. protected void ShowTargetIndicators(IEnumerable<Vector3I> cells) Parameters cells IEnumerable<Vector3I> Reachable cells per the skill's range. ShowVictoryScreen() Spawn (or refresh) the VictoryScreen overlay and bind it to the surviving party. Called from CheckWinLoseCondition on victory. protected void ShowVictoryScreen() Remarks XP and loot values are placeholders for now — the rest of the project will wire them up to the progression and loot-drop systems later. UseSkill(IUnitSystem, IUnitSystem, ISkillSystem) Uses a skill from a source unit on a target unit, depending on the skill’s target type. public virtual void UseSkill(IUnitSystem sourceUnit, IUnitSystem targetUnit, ISkillSystem skill) Parameters sourceUnit IUnitSystem The unit that performs the skill. targetUnit IUnitSystem The main target unit of the skill. skill ISkillSystem The skill being used, containing information about its target type and effects. Examples SkillSystem healSkill = new SkillSystem("Heal", TargetTypes.SingleAlly); gameManager.UseSkill(playerUnit, allyUnit, healSkill); Remarks Determines which units are allies or enemies based on the source unit, then applies the skill accordingly depending on its TargetType. If required containers are not initialized, the method prints an error and exits early. Warn(string) Post a negative-severity warning to the log (e.g. "Out of range"). protected static void Warn(string message) Parameters message string User-facing text. WireHudEvents() Connect HUD buttons to the gameplay handlers. protected void WireHudEvents() Remarks Call AFTER EnsureHud() and ONE ProcessFrame yield so the HUD's child widgets have run their _Ready. _ExitTree() Called when the node is removed from the scene tree. Cleans up the manager instance and sets it to null. public override void _ExitTree() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is removed from the scene tree. It ensures that the manager instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. This is important for preventing memory leaks and ensuring that the manager can be re-initialized later if needed. _Input(InputEvent) Called when there is an input event. The input event propagates up through the node tree until a node consumes it. It is only called if input processing is enabled, which is done automatically if this method is overridden, and can be toggled with SetProcessInput(bool). To consume the input event and stop it propagating further to other nodes, Godot.Viewport.SetInputAsHandled() can be called. For gameplay input, Godot.Node._UnhandledInput(Godot.InputEvent) and Godot.Node._UnhandledKeyInput(Godot.InputEvent) are usually a better fit as they allow the GUI to intercept the events first. Note: This method is only called if the node is present in the scene tree (i.e. if it's not an orphan). public override void _Input(InputEvent @event) Parameters event InputEvent _Ready() Called when the node is added to the scene tree. Initializes the manager instance and checks for duplicates. public override void _Ready() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It ensures that only one instance of the manager exists in the scene tree. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate. _UnhandledInput(InputEvent) Called when an Godot.InputEvent hasn't been consumed by Godot.Node._Input(Godot.InputEvent) or any GUI Godot.Control item. It is called after Godot.Node._ShortcutInput(Godot.InputEvent) and after Godot.Node._UnhandledKeyInput(Godot.InputEvent). The input event propagates up through the node tree until a node consumes it. It is only called if unhandled input processing is enabled, which is done automatically if this method is overridden, and can be toggled with SetProcessUnhandledInput(bool). To consume the input event and stop it propagating further to other nodes, Godot.Viewport.SetInputAsHandled() can be called. For gameplay input, this method is usually a better fit than Godot.Node._Input(Godot.InputEvent), as GUI events need a higher priority. For keyboard shortcuts, consider using Godot.Node._ShortcutInput(Godot.InputEvent) instead, as it is called before this method. Finally, to handle keyboard events, consider using Godot.Node._UnhandledKeyInput(Godot.InputEvent) for performance reasons. Note: This method is only called if the node is present in the scene tree (i.e. if it's not an orphan). public override void _UnhandledInput(InputEvent @event) Parameters event InputEvent

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Influenced by narrative beats outside combat (mercenary contract outcomes, dialogue choices, mission completion methods). Feeds RollBacklash(IUnitSystem, float) which modulates the chance a dark-magic cast triggers a corruption side-effect on the caster. Sign convention: negative karma = corrupt-leaning (less backlash because the user has embraced morally-grey choices); positive karma = virtuous (more backlash because dark magic is unfamiliar). Register as an AutoLoad singleton. Fields MaxKarma Inclusive maximum karma value. public const int MaxKarma = 100 Field Value int MinKarma Inclusive minimum karma value. public const int MinKarma = -100 Field Value int Properties Instance Active singleton instance, set in Initialize(). public static KarmaManager? Instance { get; } Property Value KarmaManager Karma Current karma value, clamped to [-100, +100]. public int Karma { get; } Property Value int Methods AdjustKarma(int) Adjust karma by delta (clamped after). public void AdjustKarma(int delta) Parameters delta int Signed change. Cleanup() Cleans up the manager instance. This method can be overridden in derived classes to implement specific cleanup logic. protected override void Cleanup() Remarks This method is called when the manager is removed from the scene tree. It provides a place for derived classes to implement any necessary cleanup logic, such as disconnecting signals or releasing resources. By default, it does nothing, but derived classes can override it to perform specific cleanup tasks. Initialize() Initializes the manager instance. This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the manager. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the manager's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. SetKarma(int) Set the karma value directly (clamped to legal range). public void SetKarma(int value) Parameters value int New value.

class is responsible for showing, hiding, and navigating between menus. It maintains a history of menus for back navigation and provides methods to register and unregister menus. It also emits signals when the current menu changes. Fields MAIN_MENU public const string MAIN_MENU = "main_menu" Field Value string OPTIONS_MENU public const string OPTIONS_MENU = "options_menu" Field Value string PAUSE_MENU public const string PAUSE_MENU = "pause_menu" Field Value string Properties Instance public static MenuManager? Instance { get; protected set; } Property Value MenuManager Methods ClearHistory() Clears the menu history stack. This method removes all entries from the history stack, effectively resetting it. public void ClearHistory() Remarks This method is useful when you want to reset the menu navigation state. It clears the history stack, so there are no previous menus to go back to. EmitSignalMenuChanged(string) protected void EmitSignalMenuChanged(string menuName) Parameters menuName string GetCurrentMenu() Gets the name of the current active menu. public string? GetCurrentMenu() Returns string The name of the current menu, or null if no menu is active. Remarks This method returns the name of the currently active menu. If no menu is currently active, it returns null. This can be useful for checking the current state of the menu system. GoBack() Goes back to the previous menu in the history stack. If there is no previous menu, it prints a message indicating that. public void GoBack() Remarks This method pops the last menu from the history stack and shows it. If the history stack is empty, it prints a message indicating that there is no previous menu to go back to. It does not add the previous menu back to the history stack when going back. Initialize() Initializes the MenuManager singleton instance. Ensures only one instance exists and sets up the initial state. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It checks for duplicate instances and initializes the menu system. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate. IsMenuActive(string) Checks if a specific menu is currently active. public bool IsMenuActive(string menuName) Parameters menuName string The unique name of the menu to check. Returns bool True if the specified menu is active, otherwise false. Remarks This method checks if the specified menu is currently the active menu. It compares the provided menu name with the current active menu name. This can be useful for UI logic that needs to know if a specific menu is currently displayed. RegisterMenu(string, Control) Registers a menu with the MenuManager. Adds the menu to the internal dictionary and hides it by default. public virtual void RegisterMenu(string menuName, Control menuControl) Parameters menuName string The unique name of the menu to register. menuControl Control The Control instance representing the menu. Remarks This method checks if the menu is already registered to avoid duplicates. If the menu is already registered, it prints an error message and does not add it again. It also connects back signals for navigation menus, such as OptionsMenu, to handle back navigation. SetInstanceForTesting(MenuManager?) FOR TESTING ONLY: Manually sets the singleton instance. This method should only be used in unit tests. public static void SetInstanceForTesting(MenuManager? instance) Parameters instance MenuManager The instance to set as the singleton. ShowMenu(string, bool) Shows a menu by its name. Hides the current menu and shows the specified menu. public virtual void ShowMenu(string menuName, bool addToHistory = true) Parameters menuName string The unique name of the menu to show. addToHistory bool Whether to add the current menu to the history stack for back navigation. Remarks This method checks if the specified menu exists in the registered menus. If it does, it hides the current menu and shows the specified menu. If the current menu is not empty, it adds it to the history stack for back navigation. If the specified menu is not found, it prints an error message. UnregisterMenu(string) Unregisters a menu from the MenuManager. Removes the menu from the internal dictionary. public void UnregisterMenu(string menuName) Parameters menuName string The unique name of the menu to unregister. Remarks This method checks if the menu exists before attempting to remove it. If the menu is currently active, it will hide it before removing. Events MenuChanged public event MenuManager.MenuChangedEventHandler MenuChanged Event Type MenuManager.MenuChangedEventHandler

Instance { get; } Property Value PlayerInventoryManager PartyLoadouts One persistent 5-slot loadout per party slot. Index 0 = slot for first unit, 1 = second, etc. These survive scene transitions; units read from them at battle start. public InventorySystem[] PartyLoadouts { get; } Property Value InventorySystem[] PartyNames Display names for each party slot, set when units are registered. public string[] PartyNames { get; } Property Value string[] Methods RegisterUnit(int, IUnitSystem) Register a unit into a party slot. Called by GameManager at battle start. Copies the persistent loadout into the unit's live inventory. public void RegisterUnit(int partySlot, IUnitSystem unit) Parameters partySlot int unit IUnitSystem SyncBack(int, IUnitSystem) Sync a unit's live inventory back into its persistent loadout. Called by GameManager at battle end. public void SyncBack(int partySlot, IUnitSystem unit) Parameters partySlot int unit IUnitSystem _ExitTree() Called when the node is about to leave the Godot.SceneTree (e.g. upon freeing, scene changing, or after calling Godot.Node.RemoveChild(Godot.Node) in a script). If the node has children, its Godot.Node._ExitTree() callback will be called last, after all its children have left the tree. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationExitTree notification in _Notification(int) and signal Godot.Node.TreeExiting. To get notified when the node has already left the active tree, connect to the Godot.Node.TreeExited. public override void _ExitTree() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

UiScaleDefault Default scale (100% — the design size everything else multiplies against). public const float UiScaleDefault = 1 Field Value float UiScaleMax Upper bound of UI scale. Larger and the battle HUD widgets begin to clip. public const float UiScaleMax = 1.5 Field Value float UiScaleMin Lower bound of UI scale (e.g. 50% size). Smaller than this becomes unreadable. public const float UiScaleMin = 0.5 Field Value float Properties Instance public static SettingsManager? Instance { get; } Property Value SettingsManager Methods EmitSignalDialogueSizeChanged(float) protected void EmitSignalDialogueSizeChanged(float newSize) Parameters newSize float EmitSignalInputBindingChanged(string) protected void EmitSignalInputBindingChanged(string action) Parameters action string EmitSignalSettingsChanged(string, Variant) protected void EmitSignalSettingsChanged(string settingKey, Variant newValue) Parameters settingKey string newValue Variant EmitSignalUiScaleChanged(float) protected void EmitSignalUiScaleChanged(float newScale) Parameters newScale float GetDialogueSize() Gets the current dialogue size setting. public float GetDialogueSize() Returns float The dialogue size as a float, defaulting to 1.0f if not set. Remarks This method retrieves the dialogue size from the settings data. If the dialogue size is not set, it returns a default value of 1.0f. GetInputBinding(string) Gets the current input binding for an action. public InputEvent? GetInputBinding(string action) Parameters action string The action name to retrieve the binding for. Returns InputEvent The InputEvent bound to the action, or null if not found. GetSetting<T>(string, T?) Gets a custom setting by key, with an optional default value. public T? GetSetting<T>(string key, T? defaultValue = default) Parameters key string The key of the setting to retrieve. defaultValue T The default value to return if the setting is not found. Returns T The setting value if found, otherwise the default value. Type Parameters T The type of the setting value. Remarks This method retrieves a custom setting from the settings data. If the setting is not found, it returns the provided default value. It uses JSON serialization to convert the setting value to the specified type. GetUiScale() Gets the user's UI scale multiplier — a single number every HUD widget multiplies its design font size by. 1.0 means "exactly the design size", 0.5 half, 1.5 50% larger. public float GetUiScale() Returns float The scale, clamped to UiScaleMin..UiScaleMax. Initialize() Initializes the SettingsManager singleton instance. Loads settings from the JSON file or creates a new default settings object. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It ensures that only one instance of SettingsManager exists and initializes the settings data. If the settings file does not exist, it creates a new default settings object and saves it. LoadSettings() Loads settings from the JSON file. If the file does not exist, creates a new default settings object. public virtual void LoadSettings() Remarks This method reads the settings from a JSON file located at user://settings.json. If the file is not found, it initializes a new SettingsData object with default values and saves it to create the settings file. ResetToDefaults() Resets all settings to their default values. public void ResetToDefaults() Remarks This method clears the current settings and initializes a new SettingsData object with default values. It saves the new settings to the JSON file and emits a SettingsChanged signal to notify other components. SaveSettings() Saves the current settings to the JSON file. public virtual void SaveSettings() Remarks This method serializes the current settings data to a JSON string and writes it to the user://settings.json file. It handles any exceptions that may occur during the file operations. SetDialogueSize(float) Sets the dialogue size and saves the settings. Emits a signal to notify other components of the change. public void SetDialogueSize(float size) Parameters size float The new dialogue size to set. Remarks This method updates the dialogue size in the settings data and saves it to the JSON file. It also emits a SettingsChanged signal to notify other components of the change. The size is clamped between 0.5f and 2.0f to ensure it remains within a reasonable range. SetInputBinding(string, InputEvent?) Sets a new input binding for an action and saves the settings. Emits a signal to notify other components of the change. public void SetInputBinding(string action, InputEvent? inputEvent) Parameters action string The action name to set the binding for. inputEvent InputEvent The InputEvent to bind to the action. SetSetting<T>(string, T) Sets a custom setting by key and saves the settings. Emits a signal to notify other components of the change. public void SetSetting<T>(string key, T value) Parameters key string The key of the setting to set. value T The value to set for the setting. Type Parameters T The type of the setting value. Remarks This method updates a custom setting in the settings data and saves it to the JSON file. It also emits a SettingsChanged signal to notify other components of the change. The value is serialized to JSON format before being stored. SetUiScale(float) Persists a new UI scale and emits UiScaleChanged when the value actually changes (small float deltas are absorbed). HUD code can subscribe and rebuild its widgets in response. public void SetUiScale(float scale) Parameters scale float New scale; clamped to UiScaleMin..UiScaleMax. Events DialogueSizeChanged public event SettingsManager.DialogueSizeChangedEventHandler DialogueSizeChanged Event Type SettingsManager.DialogueSizeChangedEventHandler InputBindingChanged public event SettingsManager.InputBindingChangedEventHandler InputBindingChanged Event Type SettingsManager.InputBindingChangedEventHandler SettingsChanged public event SettingsManager.SettingsChangedEventHandler SettingsChanged Event Type SettingsManager.SettingsChangedEventHandler UiScaleChanged Emitted when GetUiScale() changes. The HUD subscribes to this so widgets rescale their fonts (and rebuild their min-sizes) without needing a scene reload. public event SettingsManager.UiScaleChangedEventHandler UiScaleChanged Event Type SettingsManager.UiScaleChangedEventHandler

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks The base TurnManager distinguishes player vs enemy turns via AovDataStructures.TurnState. Allied (guest) units are still tagged with EnemyTurn internally because they're AI-driven, but this partial fires OnAllyTurn instead of OnEnemyTurn when the active unit's Faction is Ally. GameManager routes the dispatch. Properties Active Public accessor for the active TurnManager in the scene. Returns null when no battle is active. public static TurnManager? Active { get; } Property Value TurnManager CurrentUnit The unit currently taking its turn, or null if the turn order is not set up yet. public UnitSystem? CurrentUnit { get; } Property Value UnitSystem Methods EndTurnManagerLoop() Called by the GameManager to inform the TurnManager the game is finished public virtual void EndTurnManagerLoop() GetCurrentUnit() Gets the unit currently taking its turn. public IUnitSystem GetCurrentUnit() Returns IUnitSystem The IUnitSystem that is currently active. GetUpcomingUnits(int) Returns the next count alive units in activation order, starting with the currently-acting unit. Used by the HUD turn-queue widget. public IReadOnlyList<IUnitSystem> GetUpcomingUnits(int count = 8) Parameters count int Maximum number of units to return. Returns IReadOnlyList<IUnitSystem> Read-only list of upcoming units. Initialize() Initializes the TurnManager singleton instance. Ensures only one instance exists and sets up the initial state. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It checks for duplicate instances and initializes the game system. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate. InitializeTurnOrder(List<IUnitSystem>, List<IUnitSystem>) Initializes the turn order list based on all participating units. public void InitializeTurnOrder(List<IUnitSystem> playerUnits, List<IUnitSystem> enemyUnits) Parameters playerUnits List<IUnitSystem> List of all player-controlled units. enemyUnits List<IUnitSystem> List of all enemy-controlled units. Remarks The order is determined by each unit's BaseSpeed value, sorted from highest to lowest. InitializeTurnOrder(List<IUnitSystem>, List<IUnitSystem>, List<IUnitSystem>) Initialise the turn order with three factions. Allies are AI-driven friendly guests (recruited mercs, summoned creatures, scripted helpers). public void InitializeTurnOrder(List<IUnitSystem> playerUnits, List<IUnitSystem> allyUnits, List<IUnitSystem> enemyUnits) Parameters playerUnits List<IUnitSystem> Player-controlled units. allyUnits List<IUnitSystem> AI-controlled friendly units (may be empty). enemyUnits List<IUnitSystem> AI-controlled hostile units. Remarks Internally allied units are stored as EnemyTurn (so the existing turn loop dispatches to AI), but GameManager consults Faction to fire the right HUD events. RaiseAllyTurn(bool) Dispatch helper for GameManager — fires OnAllyTurn / OnAllyTurnEnd when an AI-faction turn belongs to an ally rather than an enemy. public void RaiseAllyTurn(bool isStart) Parameters isStart bool True for turn start, false for turn end. RequestAbort(BattleOutcome) Request the battle loop to terminate with a specific outcome. public void RequestAbort(BattleOutcome outcome) Parameters outcome BattleOutcome Outcome to surface to the caller. RunBattleLoop() Run the battle loop and return the final result. public Task<BattleResult> RunBattleLoop() Returns Task<BattleResult> The outcome of the battle once the loop terminates. Remarks Bridges StartBattle() (the legacy entry point) to the BattleResult contract that BattleLauncher expects. SetAIManager(EnemyAIManager) Sets the reference to the EnemyAIManager instance. public void SetAIManager(EnemyAIManager aiManager) Parameters aiManager EnemyAIManager The EnemyAIManager instance to use. SetVictoryCondition(VictoryCondition?) Install the victory condition the battle loop should evaluate each turn. public void SetVictoryCondition(VictoryCondition? condition) Parameters condition VictoryCondition Condition implementation, or null to clear. StartBattle() Starts the turn-based battle loop asynchronously. public Task StartBattle() Returns Task A task representing the battle loop’s lifetime. _ExitTree() Called when the node is removed from the scene tree. Cleans up the manager instance and sets it to null. public override void _ExitTree() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is removed from the scene tree. It ensures that the manager instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. This is important for preventing memory leaks and ensuring that the manager can be re-initialized later if needed. Events OnAllyTurn Triggered when an Ally-faction unit's turn begins. public event Action? OnAllyTurn Event Type Action OnAllyTurnEnd Triggered when an Ally-faction unit's turn ends. public event Action? OnAllyTurnEnd Event Type Action OnCurrentTurnEnd Triggered when the current turn ends. public event Action? OnCurrentTurnEnd Event Type Action OnEnemyTurn Triggered when the enemy's turn begins. public event Action? OnEnemyTurn Event Type Action OnEnemyTurnEnd Triggered when the enemy's turn ends public event Action? OnEnemyTurnEnd Event Type Action OnPlayerTurn Triggered when the player's turn begins. public event Action? OnPlayerTurn Event Type Action OnPlayerTurnEnd Triggered when the player's turn ends. public event Action? OnPlayerTurnEnd Event Type Action

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Managers.html.summary: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Managers Classes AudioManager Centralised audio service for music, ambient layers and one-shot SFX. Implements IAudioService from the Core layer and bridges Godot audio APIs to the rest of the game. BaseManager Base class for all manager singletons in the game. Implements the Manager Pattern from your architecture documentation. BattleLauncher Autoload that owns the exploration ↔ battle transition. EnemyAIManager GameManager GameManager partial — indicator updates, hover ray-cast, cancel, warnings, A* tween-animated movement, and the HUD event handlers wired by GameManagerHud.WireHudEvents. KarmaManager Holds the player party's global karma value in [-100, +100]. MenuManager Manages menu navigation and state. Coordinates between different menus following the Manager Pattern. PlayerInventoryManager SceneManager The SceneManager handles every inside level related informations. SettingsData Data structure for settings storage SettingsManager Manages game settings and provides centralized access to configuration options. Follows the Manager Pattern from your architecture. State Base class for all states used in a StateMachine. Provides a lifecycle and hooks for behavior implementation. StateMachine Represents a finite state machine that manages transitions between different states. Each child node of this node is expected to be a State. TurnManager TurnManager partial — adds three-faction support, an upcoming-queue getter for the HUD turn-order widget, and an OnAllyTurn event.
