object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Sits under the AlliedUnits container so GameManager.LoadUnits tags it with Ally. The player cannot select or steer this unit — it acts on the OnAllyTurn event via an EnemyAIBehavior child node, picking targets from whatever the current battle's hostile-faction list contains. Personality is Aggressive so the merc closes in on enemies rather than holding back. Loadout mirrors the Fighter archetype minus the buff utility — pure brawler. Methods Initialize() Initializes the unit instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the unit's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic.

Both Player1 and Player2 are part of the player party and act on Player turns. The Swordsman skill catalogue (Phase B) drives the active slots; passives are wired but inert until the damage formula reads them. Methods Initialize() Initializes the unit instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected override void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Bat.title: Enum BattleNotifications.Severity | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Bat.summary: Enum BattleNotifications.Severity Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Severity tag — the HUD usually colour-codes the line based on this. public enum BattleNotifications.Severity Fields Critical = 3 Critical event (death, defeat, hard error). Info = 0 Neutral message (turn started, move complete, etc.). Negative = 2 Negative event (damage, debuff, blocked action). Positive = 1 Positive event (heal, buff, victory).

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Bat.title: Class BattleNotifications | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Bat.summary: Class BattleNotifications Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Simple decoupled notification hub used to surface user-facing battle messages (warnings, info, deaths, etc.) without coupling producers to the HUD widgets. public static class BattleNotifications Inheritance object BattleNotifications Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks A producer such as GameManager calls Post(string, Severity) when it needs to tell the player something — for example "Target out of range" or "Not enough mana". Any subscriber (typically BattleLog) receives the message and renders it. Pure C# (no Godot dependencies) so the producer logic can be unit-tested without instantiating the HUD. Methods Clear() Remove every subscriber. Call when transitioning out of a battle scene to avoid dangling delegates pointing at freed nodes. public static void Clear() Post(string, Severity) Push a new notification to every subscriber. public static void Post(string message, BattleNotifications.Severity severity = Severity.Info) Parameters message string User-facing text. severity BattleNotifications.Severity How to colour-code the line. Defaults to Info. Events Posted Fires whenever a producer pushes a new notification. Subscribers MUST unsubscribe in their cleanup path to avoid leaks. public static event Action<string, BattleNotifications.Severity>? Posted Event Type Action<string, BattleNotifications.Severity>

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Bat.title: Class BattleSetup | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Bat.summary: Class BattleSetup Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Declarative description of an encounter — who fights, on which map, and where to return when the battle resolves. Built by the exploration layer (an NpcBattleTrigger on a story-quest NPC, a procedural encounter generator, etc.) and handed to BattleLauncher. public sealed class BattleSetup Inheritance object BattleSetup Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Designed to be reusable: the same battle scene can host many different encounters by swapping the EnemyUnits list. The fields are init-only properties so a setup is immutable once constructed — BattleLauncher stores the active setup and reads from it; nothing should mutate it mid-battle. ReturnScenePath + ReturnPosition are how the "Forfeit" flow gets the player back to where they were when they triggered the fight. Treat it as a temporary checkpoint — not a save file. If you need persistent save behaviour, layer a real save system on top. Properties AllyUnits AI-controlled friendly guests (recruited mercs, summoned creatures, scripted helpers). Spawned into AlliedUnits. public List<PackedScene> AllyUnits { get; init; } Property Value List<PackedScene> BattleScene The battle scene to load (a .tscn with a GameManager, GridMap, and the empty PlayerUnits / AlliedUnits / EnemyUnits containers ready to receive instantiated units). public PackedScene? BattleScene { get; init; } Property Value PackedScene EncounterName Display name shown in the battle banner / log header. Optional. public string EncounterName { get; init; } Property Value string EnemyUnits The hostile units the encounter spawns. Spawned into EnemyUnits. public List<PackedScene> EnemyUnits { get; init; } Property Value List<PackedScene> PlayerUnits PackedScenes for each player-controlled unit (the active party). Each scene's root must extend UnitSystem. The launcher instantiates one of each and drops them into the battle scene's PlayerUnits container. public List<PackedScene> PlayerUnits { get; init; } Property Value List<PackedScene> ReturnPosition World position to spawn the player at on return. Typically the position the player had when they triggered the encounter — captured by the NpcBattleTrigger at Trigger time. public Vector3 ReturnPosition { get; init; } Property Value Vector3 ReturnScenePath res:// path of the scene to return to once the battle ends (forfeit or post-victory). Empty string disables the return flow — the launcher will just stay on the battle scene's end-screen. public string ReturnScenePath { get; init; } Property Value string

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Spawned by GameManager for every loaded unit. Blue = Player, green = Ally, red = Enemy. The arrow additionally pulses brighter while the parent unit is the currently-acting one, so the player can identify whose turn it is from any camera angle. Pure presentation node — has no gameplay state. Lives as a child of the unit's Godot.CharacterBody3D so it inherits its world transform automatically, then floats HoverHeight units above the unit's origin. Fields HoverHeight How far above the unit the arrow floats, in world units. public const float HoverHeight = 1.2 Field Value float Size Triangle (arrow) edge length, in world units. public const float Size = 0.35 Field Value float Methods Bind(Faction) Configure the marker for a given faction. Call once after AddChild. public void Bind(Faction faction) Parameters faction Faction Faction the parent unit belongs to. SetActive(bool) Toggle whether the marker should pulse brightly to indicate the active turn. public void SetActive(bool active) Parameters active bool True when the parent unit is currently acting. _Process(double) Called on each idle frame, prior to rendering, and after physics ticks have been processed. delta is the time between frames in seconds. It is only called if processing is enabled for this Node, which is done automatically if this method is overridden, and can be toggled with SetProcess(bool). Processing happens in order of Godot.Node.ProcessPriority, lower priority values are called first. Nodes with the same priority are processed in tree order, or top to bottom as seen in the editor (also known as pre-order traversal). Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationProcess notification in _Notification(int). Note: This method is only called if the node is present in the scene tree (i.e. if it's not an orphan). Note: When the engine is struggling and the frame rate is lowered, delta will increase. When delta is increased, it's capped at a maximum of Godot.Engine.TimeScale * Godot.Engine.MaxPhysicsStepsPerFrame / Godot.Engine.PhysicsTicksPerSecond. As a result, accumulated delta may not represent real world time. Note: When --fixed-fps is enabled or the engine is running in Movie Maker mode (see Godot.MovieWriter), process delta will always be the same for every frame, regardless of how much time the frame took to render. Note: Frame delta may be post-processed by Godot.OS.DeltaSmoothing if this is enabled for the project. public override void _Process(double delta) Parameters delta double _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

MapSystem and call Initialize(MapSystem) once. From then on, callers use the high-level methods (ShowMoveTiles(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)>), ShowTargetTiles(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)>), ShowHover((int X, int Y, int Z)?), HideAll()) without juggling three mesh pools. RaycastCell(Camera3D, Vector2) is colocated here because every caller that asks for a hover update needs both the camera ray and the indicator update. Properties Map The map this overlay was bound to. public MapSystem? Map { get; } Property Value MapSystem Methods HideAll() Hide all three overlays at once. public void HideAll() Initialize(MapSystem) Spawn the three child indicators and bind them to map. public void Initialize(MapSystem map) Parameters map MapSystem The active battle map. RaycastCell(Camera3D camera, Vector2 screenPos) Parameters camera Camera3D The active 3D camera. screenPos Vector2 Mouse position in viewport coordinates. Returns Vector3I? The cell or null when the ray missed any collider. ShowHover((int X, int Y, int Z)?) Light a single yellow tile for hover feedback. Pass null to clear. public void ShowHover((int X, int Y, int Z)? cell) Parameters cell (int, int, int)? Cell to highlight, or null to hide. object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Used for move-tile, skill-target and hover overlays. A pool of mesh instances is maintained internally and reused between calls so the indicator can be flipped on and off many times per turn without alloc churn. Bind once with Bind(MapSystem) after the map exists, then call Show(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)>) with the cells to highlight or Hide() to clear. Properties Height Vertical offset above the cell so the quad isn't z-fighting with the floor. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public float Height { get; set; } Property Value float TileColor Translucent fill colour. Defaults to a friendly green. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public Color TileColor { get; set; } Property Value Color Methods Bind(MapSystem) Attach the indicator to a map. Must be called before Show(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)>). public void Bind(MapSystem map) Parameters map MapSystem The active MapSystem. Hide() Hide every indicator. public void Hide() Show(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)>) Display indicators on every tile in tiles, hiding extras. public void Show(IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)> tiles) Parameters tiles IReadOnlyList<(int X, int Y, int Z)> Cell coordinates to highlight.

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Designer flow:
  1. Add an NPC (with whatever sprite / collider it needs) to the world scene.
  2. Add this script as a child node, fill in the inspector exports (battle scene, enemies, party, allies).
  3. From the player controller, call on the closest when an interact key is pressed.
Trigger(Node3D) captures the player's current scene path + world position as the "return point", builds a BattleSetup, and calls Launch(BattleSetup). If the player loses and presses Forfeit on the GameOverScreen, BattleLauncher sends them back to that exact scene + position so they can talk to the NPC again or walk away. Properties AllyUnits Optional friendly AI guests this NPC brings along (recruited mercs, etc). [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public Array AllyUnits { get; set; } Property Value Array BattleScene Battle scene template — typically res://scenes/Test/Test.tscn or similar. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public PackedScene? BattleScene { get; set; } Property Value PackedScene EncounterName Display name shown on the battle banner / log header. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public string EncounterName { get; set; } Property Value string EnemyUnits Hostile units this NPC will spawn into EnemyUnits. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public Array EnemyUnits { get; set; } Property Value Array PartyOverride Player party scenes. Usually wired by a global PartyManager rather than hardcoded per NPC, but kept as an inspector option for one-off encounters where the encounter forces a specific lineup. [Export(PropertyHint.None, "")] public Array PartyOverride { get; set; } Property Value Array Methods Trigger(Node3D) Build a BattleSetup from this NPC's exported lists, capture the player's current world location as the return point, and launch. public void Trigger(Node3D player) Parameters player Node3D The player Node3D — used to capture the return position. Events BattleTriggered Fired right before the scene transition kicks off. Listeners (sound effects, screen-fade tween, NPC dialogue close, …) hook here. public event NpcBattleTrigger.BattleTriggeredEventHandler BattleTriggered Event Type NpcBattleTrigger.BattleTriggeredEventHandler

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Pat.title: Class Pathfinder | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.Pat.summary: Class Pathfinder Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Stateless grid path-finder. Runs BFS with parent tracking on an IMapSystem to find the shortest walkable path between two cells. public static class Pathfinder Inheritance object Pathfinder Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Pure C# — does not touch Godot. Drop into the Core test project as-is and exercise it with a fake IMapSystem implementation. BFS rather than full A* because the grid is uniform-cost; on uniform-cost grids BFS already returns optimal paths and avoids the heuristic-tuning surface. Swap in A* later if non-uniform terrain is added. The neighbour set is the four cardinal X/Z directions on the same Y. Vertical traversal (stairs / ramps) is intentionally not modelled here — extend by passing a custom neighbour function if your map needs it. Methods FindPath(IMapSystem, (int X, int Y, int Z), (int X, int Y, int Z)) Find the shortest walkable path from from to to. public static List<(int X, int Y, int Z)>? FindPath(IMapSystem map, (int X, int Y, int Z) from, (int X, int Y, int Z) to) Parameters map IMapSystem The map to query for walkability. from (int, int, int) Starting cell (inclusive). to (int, int, int) Destination cell (inclusive). Returns List<(int, int, int)> The list of cells the unit walks through, ordered from the first step to the destination (start is NOT included). Returns an empty list when start == destination. Returns null when no path exists. ManhattanDistance((int X, int Y, int Z), (int X, int Y, int Z)) Compute the Manhattan distance between two cells on the X/Z plane. Used by callers as a cheap range check before invoking FindPath(IMapSystem, (int X, int Y, int Z), (int X, int Y, int Z)). public static int ManhattanDistance((int X, int Y, int Z) a, (int X, int Y, int Z) b) Parameters a (int, int, int) First cell. b (int, int, int) Second cell. Returns int The unsigned X+Z step count between the two cells.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.htm.title: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle.htm.summary: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Battle Classes BattleNotifications Simple decoupled notification hub used to surface user-facing battle messages (warnings, info, deaths, etc.) without coupling producers to the HUD widgets. BattleSetup Declarative description of an encounter — who fights, on which map, and where to return when the battle resolves. Built by the exploration layer (an NpcBattleTrigger on a story-quest NPC, a procedural encounter generator, etc.) and handed to BattleLauncher. FactionMarker Small downward-pointing arrow that floats above a unit, colour-coded by faction. IndicatorOverlay Bundles three world-space tile overlays — move (green), skill target (red), hover (yellow) — under a single facade. MoveIndicator World-space overlay that draws a translucent quad above each cell in a list. NpcBattleTrigger Drop this on an NPC node in an exploration scene to make that NPC launch a configurable battle when the player interacts with them. Pathfinder Stateless grid path-finder. Runs BFS with parent tracking on an IMapSystem to find the shortest walkable path between two cells. Enums BattleNotifications.Severity Severity tag — the HUD usually colour-codes the line based on this.

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks BattleInputSystem captures and interprets user actions (keyboard and mouse) related to combat gameplay, including: Passing the turn Moving a unit or selecting a target on the map Selecting the move action Selecting a skill This system acts as a single entry point for battle-related inputs and communicates with other game systems through Godot signals. The class follows a singleton-like pattern to ensure that only one active instance exists within the scene tree. Methods EmitSignalOnMoveUnitOrSelectTargetPressed(Vector3I) protected void EmitSignalOnMoveUnitOrSelectTargetPressed(Vector3I dest) Parameters dest Vector3I EmitSignalOnOpenInventoryPressed() protected void EmitSignalOnOpenInventoryPressed() EmitSignalOnPassTurnPressed() protected void EmitSignalOnPassTurnPressed() EmitSignalOnSelectMovePressed() protected void EmitSignalOnSelectMovePressed() EmitSignalOnSelectedSkillPressed(int) protected void EmitSignalOnSelectedSkillPressed(int skillId) Parameters skillId int EmitSignalOnUseItemPressed(int) protected void EmitSignalOnUseItemPressed(int slotIndex) Parameters slotIndex int Initialize() Initializes the BattleInputSystem instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected virtual void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the map's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. SetInputEnabled(bool) Set the input to enabled or not. public virtual void SetInputEnabled(bool enabled) Parameters enabled bool A boolean that set the input to enabled or not. SetInstanceForTesting(BattleInputSystem?) FOR TESTING ONLY: Manually sets the singleton instance. This method should only be used in unit tests. protected static void SetInstanceForTesting(BattleInputSystem? instance) Parameters instance BattleInputSystem The instance to set as the singleton. _ExitTree() Called when the node is removed from the scene tree. Cleans up the BattleInputSystem instance and sets it to null. public override void _ExitTree() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is removed from the scene tree. It ensures that the BattleInputSystem instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. This is important for preventing memory leaks and ensuring that the manager can be re-initialized later if needed. _Input(InputEvent) Called when there is an input event. The input event propagates up through the node tree until a node consumes it. It is only called if input processing is enabled, which is done automatically if this method is overridden, and can be toggled with SetProcessInput(bool). To consume the input event and stop it propagating further to other nodes, Godot.Viewport.SetInputAsHandled() can be called. For gameplay input, Godot.Node._UnhandledInput(Godot.InputEvent) and Godot.Node._UnhandledKeyInput(Godot.InputEvent) are usually a better fit as they allow the GUI to intercept the events first. Note: This method is only called if the node is present in the scene tree (i.e. if it's not an orphan). public override void _Input(InputEvent @event) Parameters event InputEvent _Ready() Called when the node is added to the scene tree. Initializes the BattleInputSystem instance and checks for duplicates. public override void _Ready() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It ensures that only one instance of the BattleInputSystem exists in the scene tree. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate. Events OnMoveUnitOrSelectTargetPressed Emitted when the player clicks on a map cell to move a unit or select a target. public event BattleInputSystem.OnMoveUnitOrSelectTargetPressedEventHandler OnMoveUnitOrSelectTargetPressed Event Type BattleInputSystem.OnMoveUnitOrSelectTargetPressedEventHandler Remarks This signal notifies systems responsible for unit movement or selection that the player has requested a move or target action. OnOpenInventoryPressed public event BattleInputSystem.OnOpenInventoryPressedEventHandler OnOpenInventoryPressed Event Type BattleInputSystem.OnOpenInventoryPressedEventHandler OnPassTurnPressed Emitted when the player presses the "pass turn" input action. public event BattleInputSystem.OnPassTurnPressedEventHandler OnPassTurnPressed Event Type BattleInputSystem.OnPassTurnPressedEventHandler Remarks Used by the GameManager to indicate that the player has chosen to skip their current turn and let control pass to the next entity. OnSelectMovePressed Emitted when the player selects the move action public event BattleInputSystem.OnSelectMovePressedEventHandler OnSelectMovePressed Event Type BattleInputSystem.OnSelectMovePressedEventHandler Remarks This signal is used to notify systems handling unit commands that the player has initiated a move selection. OnSelectedSkillPressed Emitted when the player selects a specific skill. public event BattleInputSystem.OnSelectedSkillPressedEventHandler OnSelectedSkillPressed Event Type BattleInputSystem.OnSelectedSkillPressedEventHandler Remarks Used by the combat system or skill management system to determine which skill the player intends to use. Skills are indexed from 0 to 4. OnUseItemPressed public event BattleInputSystem.OnUseItemPressedEventHandler OnUseItemPressed Event Type BattleInputSystem.OnUseItemPressedEventHandler

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Faction.ht.title: Enum Faction | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.Faction.ht.summary: Enum Faction Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Identifies which side a combatant fights for. public enum Faction Extension Methods FactionExtensions.IsAiControlled(Faction) FactionExtensions.IsFriendlyTo(Faction, Faction) FactionExtensions.IsHostileTo(Faction, Faction) Fields Ally = 1 AI-controlled friendly guest units (recruited mercs, summons, scripted helpers). Enemy = 2 AI-controlled hostile units. Player = 0 The player-controlled party. Remarks Three factions so a "guest" friendly unit (a recruited mercenary fighting alongside the player but not part of the persistent party) can be modelled separately from the player party itself. Player and Ally both treat Enemy as hostile; only Player units are driven by user input. Lives in AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems rather than AovDataStructures because it is fundamentally part of the combat system contract — units, turn manager and HUD all read it.

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.IStatusEff.title: Interface IStatusEffectBehavior | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.IStatusEff.summary: Interface IStatusEffectBehavior Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Defines the behavior contract for status effects applied to an entity. public interface IStatusEffectBehavior Remarks IStatusEffectBehavior is implemented by classes that react to status effect events such as damage, healing, stat modifiers, control effects, or revival. Each method is called by the status effect or combat system when a specific effect-related event occurs, allowing custom logic to be executed (visual feedback, stat updates, triggers, etc.). Methods OnEffectControlApplied() Called when a control effect (e.g. stun, silence, root) is applied to the entity. void OnEffectControlApplied() OnEffectControlRemoved() Called when a control effect is removed from the entity. void OnEffectControlRemoved() OnEffectDamage(ModifierType, float) Called when a damage effect is applied to the entity. void OnEffectDamage(AovDataStructures.ModifierType modifierType, float amount) Parameters modifierType AovDataStructures.ModifierType The type of modifier responsible for the damage (e.g. Flat, Percent). amount float The amount of damage applied. OnEffectHeal(float) Called when a healing effect is applied to the entity. void OnEffectHeal(float amount) Parameters amount float The amount of health restored. OnEffectModifierApplied(StatTypeWithModifier, ModifierType, float) Called when a stat modifier effect is applied to the entity. void OnEffectModifierApplied(AovDataStructures.StatTypeWithModifier statTypeWithModifier, AovDataStructures.ModifierType modifierType, float amount) Parameters statTypeWithModifier AovDataStructures.StatTypeWithModifier The affected stat. modifierType AovDataStructures.ModifierType The type of modifier applied. amount float The value of the applied modifier. OnEffectModifierRemoved(StatTypeWithModifier, ModifierType, float) Called when a stat modifier effect is removed from the entity. void OnEffectModifierRemoved(AovDataStructures.StatTypeWithModifier statTypeWithModifier, AovDataStructures.ModifierType modifierType, float amount) Parameters statTypeWithModifier AovDataStructures.StatTypeWithModifier The affected stat. modifierType AovDataStructures.ModifierType The type of modifier being removed. amount float The value of the removed modifier. OnEffectRevive(ModifierType, float) Called when a revive effect is applied to the entity. void OnEffectRevive(AovDataStructures.ModifierType modifierType, float amount) Parameters modifierType AovDataStructures.ModifierType The type of modifier responsible for the revive effect. amount float The amount of health restored upon revival.

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks This class extends Godot's Godot.GridMap and manages the grid layout, grid metadata (CellInformation), and unit placement. It enforces a single active instance through the Instance property. Fields CellsInformation List of information of every grid public readonly List<CellInformation> CellsInformation Field Value List<CellInformation> Properties Depth The total Z axis of the map (in number of cells) public int Depth { get; } Property Value int Height The total Y axis of the map (in number of cells) public int Height { get; } Property Value int Instance Instance of a map system. public static MapSystem? Instance { get; protected set; } Property Value MapSystem Remarks It will be used to check if there is only one instance. Width The total X axis of the map (in number of cells) public int Width { get; } Property Value int Methods Cleanup() Cleans up the map instance. This method can be overridden in derived classes to implement specific cleanup logic. protected virtual void Cleanup() Remarks This method is called when the manager is removed from the scene tree. It provides a place for derived classes to implement any necessary cleanup logic, such as disconnecting signals or releasing resources. By default, it does nothing, but derived classes can override it to perform specific cleanup tasks. GetCellType((int, int, int)) public virtual AovDataStructures.CellType GetCellType((int, int, int) position) Parameters position (int, int, int) Returns AovDataStructures.CellType GetUnitAt(int, int, int) Gets the unit currently occupying map cell at the given position. public virtual IUnitSystem? GetUnitAt(int x, int y, int z) Parameters x int The x-coordinate of the map cell. y int The y-coordinate of the map cell. z int The z-coordinate of the map cell. Returns IUnitSystem The unit at the specified map cell, or null if the cell is empty. GetUnitPosition(IUnitSystem) Gets the unit currently position on map. public virtual (int, int, int)? GetUnitPosition(IUnitSystem unit) Parameters unit IUnitSystem The unit to find. Returns (int, int, int)? The (x, y, z) position of the unit at the specified map, or null if the unit is not found. Initialize() Initializes the map instance This method should be overridden in derived classes to set up specific functionality. protected virtual void Initialize() Remarks This method is called by the _Ready method to initialize the map. It should contain the logic necessary to set up the map's state and functionality. Derived classes must implement this method to provide their specific initialization logic. InjectDependencies(StatusEffectSystem) Injects an instance of the status effect system into this map. public virtual void InjectDependencies(StatusEffectSystem statusEffectSystem) Parameters statusEffectSystem StatusEffectSystem The status effect system to be used by this map. IsEmpty(int, int, int) Getter to know if a cell is empty. public virtual bool IsEmpty(int x, int y, int z) Parameters x int The x-axis y int The y-axis z int The z-axis Returns bool True if the cell is empty, False otherwise IsWalkable(int, int, int) Getter to know if a cell is walkable. public virtual bool IsWalkable(int x, int y, int z) Parameters x int The x-axis y int The y-axis z int The z-axis Returns bool True if the cell is walkable, False otherwise MoveUnit(IUnitSystem, int, int, int) Moves a unit to a new position in the map. public virtual void MoveUnit(IUnitSystem unit, int newX, int newY, int newZ) Parameters unit IUnitSystem The unit to move. newX int The target x-coordinate in the cell. newY int The target y-coordinate in the cell. newZ int The target z-coordinate in the cell. Remarks Implementations should handle removing the unit from its current cell and assigning it to the new cell. This method does not check whether the target cell is walkable, which should be verified beforehand. PlaceUnits(List<IUnitSystem>, List<IUnitSystem>) Place every unit in the map. public abstract void PlaceUnits(List<IUnitSystem> playerUnits, List<IUnitSystem> enemyUnits) Parameters playerUnits List<IUnitSystem> List of every unit of the player enemyUnits List<IUnitSystem> List of every enemy on the maps Remarks It must be called only for the class initialization. RemoveUnit(int, int, int) Removes any unit present at the given map cell. public virtual void RemoveUnit(int x, int y, int z) Parameters x int The x-coordinate of the map cell. y int The y-coordinate of the map cell. z int The z-coordinate of the map cell. Remarks Implementations should clear the reference to the unit from the cell. If the cell is already empty, this method does nothing. SetStatusEffectOnCells(List<(int, int, int)>, StatusEffect<CellInformation>) Applies a status effect to a list of cells on the map. public virtual void SetStatusEffectOnCells(List<(int, int, int)> cells, StatusEffect<CellInformation> statusEffect) Parameters cells List<(int, int, int)> A list of cell coordinates represented as tuples (x, y, z) where the status effect should be applied. statusEffect StatusEffect<CellInformation> The status effect to apply to the cells. SetWalkable(int, int, int) Set the IsWalkable variable of a CellInformation to the reverse value. public virtual void SetWalkable(int x, int y, int z) Parameters x int The x-axis y int The y-axis z int The z-axis _ExitTree() Called when the node is removed from the scene tree. Cleans up the manager instance and sets it to null. public override void _ExitTree() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is removed from the scene tree. It ensures that the map instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. This is important for preventing memory leaks and ensuring that the manager can be re-initialized later if needed. _Ready() Called when the node is added to the scene tree. Initializes the map instance and checks for duplicates. public override void _Ready() Remarks This method is called automatically by Godot when the node is ready. It ensures that only one instance of the map exists in the scene tree. If a duplicate instance is found, it removes the duplicate.

Specific skills should inherit from this class and implement the Use(IUnitSystem, List<IUnitSystem>, IMapSystem?) method. Properties AreaEffect The cells affected relative to the target position (area of effect). public List<(int, int, int)> AreaEffect { get; protected init; } Property Value List<(int, int, int)> Cooldown The remaining cooldown (in turns) before the skill becomes available again. public int Cooldown { get; protected set; } Property Value int Description A description of the skill, used for tooltips or UI. public string Description { get; protected init; } Property Value string EffectType The type of effect this skill applies (e.g., damage, heal, buff). public AovDataStructures.EffectType EffectType { get; protected init; } Property Value AovDataStructures.EffectType MagicType The magical element type of this skill (e.g., Fire, Water, Light). public AovDataStructures.MagicType MagicType { get; protected init; } Property Value AovDataStructures.MagicType ManaCost The amount of mana consumed when using this skill. public float ManaCost { get; protected init; } Property Value float Name The name of the skill. public string Name { get; protected init; } Property Value string Range The maximum distance (in grid units) from which the skill can target. public int Range { get; protected init; } Property Value int TargetType The type of target(s) this skill can be used on. public AovDataStructures.TargetTypes TargetType { get; protected init; } Property Value AovDataStructures.TargetTypes TotalCooldown The total cooldown duration (in turns) before the skill can be reused. public int TotalCooldown { get; protected init; } Property Value int Methods IsTargetCellValid(IUnitSystem, int, int, int, IMapSystem) Optional cell-level targeting predicate. Default returns true — any cell in Range can be targeted. Skills with extra rules (cardinal-only charges, line-of-sight, cone area) override this to filter the red target tiles before the player ever clicks. Called by GameManager.PlayerSelectedSkill. public virtual bool IsTargetCellValid(IUnitSystem caster, int x, int y, int z, IMapSystem map) Parameters caster IUnitSystem Unit that's about to cast this skill. x int Target cell X. y int Target cell Y. z int Target cell Z. map IMapSystem Active map. Returns bool true if the cell is a legal target for this skill. Executes the skill logic when cast. public abstract void Use(IUnitSystem caster, List<IUnitSystem> targets, IMapSystem? map) Parameters caster IUnitSystem targets List<IUnitSystem> map IMapSystem Remarks This method must be implemented in derived classes to define the actual effect of the skill (damage, healing, etc.).

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.StatusEffe.summary: Class StatusEffect<TTarget> Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Base class for all status effects that can be applied to an IEffectTarget<TTarget>. public abstract class StatusEffect<TTarget> : IStatusEffect Type Parameters TTarget The type of target this status effect can be applied to, such as IUnitSystem or IMapSystem. Inheritance object StatusEffect<TTarget> Implements IStatusEffect Derived AtkBuffer BurningCellEffect BurningEffect CorruptedTransformationEffect CorruptionLevelEffect Stun TestConcreteStatusEffect<TTarget> Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This class provides core properties and methods for a status effect, including stacking behavior, duration management, and hooks for when the effect is applied, removed, or updated each turn. Constructors StatusEffect(string, string, int, bool, ModifierType, float) Base class for all status effects that can be applied to an IEffectTarget<TTarget>. protected StatusEffect(string name, string description, int duration, bool isStackable, AovDataStructures.ModifierType modifierType = ModifierType.Flat, float amount = 0) Parameters name string description string duration int isStackable bool modifierType AovDataStructures.ModifierType amount float Remarks This class provides core properties and methods for a status effect, including stacking behavior, duration management, and hooks for when the effect is applied, removed, or updated each turn. Properties Amount The amount to apply public float Amount { get; } Property Value float Description A description of what this effect does. public string Description { get; protected set; } Property Value string Duration The number of turns the effect will last. A value of -1 means the effect is permanent. public int Duration { get; protected set; } Property Value int EffectToSpread Store a effect status that can be spread public IStatusEffect? EffectToSpread { get; protected init; } Property Value IStatusEffect IsPurifiable Whether this effect can be removed by a "purify" / cleanse skill or item. public virtual bool IsPurifiable { get; } Property Value bool Remarks Per the design's status table, every status except Curse is purifiable. Override to return false on permanent / curse-style effects so they are skipped by cleanse routines. IsStackable Whether this effect can be stacked. public bool IsStackable { get; } Property Value bool ModifierType The modifier type (flat/percent) public AovDataStructures.ModifierType ModifierType { get; } Property Value AovDataStructures.ModifierType Name The display name of the effect. public string Name { get; protected set; } Property Value string ShouldApplyTwice Whether this effect should be applied twice on initial application. Used for control effects like stuns that need immediate double application. public virtual bool ShouldApplyTwice { get; } Property Value bool StackCount The number of times this effect has been stacked. public int StackCount { get; } Property Value int Methods AddStack() Increases the stack count of the effect if it is stackable. public virtual void AddStack() OnApply(TTarget) Called when this effect is applied to a target. Override this to implement cleanup logic (e.g., removing buffs, stopping visual/audio feedback). public virtual void OnRemove(TTarget target) Parameters target TTarget The target losing the effect. OnTurnPassed(TTarget) Called at the end of each turn to update the effect’s duration or apply ongoing logic. public virtual void OnTurnPassed(TTarget target) Parameters target TTarget The target affected by this effect. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.StatusEffe.title: Class StatusEffectSystem | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.StatusEffe.summary: Class StatusEffectSystem Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.Core.dll Manages the lifecycle and application of StatusEffect<TTarget>s for all tracked targets in the game, including units and map cells. public sealed class StatusEffectSystem Inheritance object StatusEffectSystem Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks This system keeps track of every target that has active status effects, applies new effects, handles stacking, and processes end-of-turn updates. Targets must implement IEffectTarget<TTarget> and be either a IUnitSystem or a IMapSystem. Methods ApplyEffect<TTarget>(TTarget, StatusEffect<TTarget>) Applies a new status effect to the given target, or stacks it if already present. public void ApplyEffect<TTarget>(TTarget target, StatusEffect<TTarget> newEffect) where TTarget : IEffectTarget<TTarget> Parameters target TTarget The target on which to apply the status effect. newEffect StatusEffect<TTarget> The StatusEffect<TTarget> to apply. Type Parameters TTarget The type of the target, must be either IUnitSystem or IMapSystem. Remarks If the target already has an effect of the same type and it is stackable, the effect will be stacked instead of being applied again. ProcessTurnEnd() Processes end-of-turn updates for all tracked targets. public void ProcessTurnEnd() Remarks Iterates over all tracked targets in AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.StatusEffectSystem._allTargets and updates their status effects. Expired effects are removed, and targets with no remaining effects are removed from the tracking list. Only targets of type CellInformation are processed by this method. ProcessUnitStatusEffects(IUnitSystem?) Processes status effect updates for a single target. public void ProcessUnitStatusEffects(IUnitSystem? target) Parameters target IUnitSystem The target whose status effects should be updated. Remarks Each status effect has its OnTurnPassed(TTarget) method called, and expired effects are removed automatically. If a target has no more active effects, it is removed from the tracking list.

class provides fundamental behavior for all combat units, including: Base stats (HP, attack, defense, etc.) Turn logic (HasPlayed) Movement logic (BFS pathfinding in 3D) Integration with MapSystem and StatusEffect<TTarget> Fields CorruptionPointsPerLevel Number of corruption points required to advance one corruption level. public const int CorruptionPointsPerLevel = 4 Field Value int MaxCorruptionLevel Maximum corruption level. Per the feature document. public const int MaxCorruptionLevel = 3 Field Value int Properties ActiveSkills List of active (usable) skills available to this unit. public List<ISkillSystem> ActiveSkills { get; protected set; } Property Value List<ISkillSystem> BaseAtk The base physical attack value of the unit. public float BaseAtk { get; protected set; } Property Value float BaseDef The base physical defense value of the unit. public float BaseDef { get; protected set; } Property Value float BaseSpeed The unit's base speed (used for turn order or initiative). public float BaseSpeed { get; protected set; } Property Value float CharacterSprite The 3D Sprite of the unit displayed in the UI. public Sprite3D? CharacterSprite { get; protected set; } Property Value Sprite3D CorruptionLevel Persistent corruption tier (0..3). 0 = clean, 3 = transformed. public int CorruptionLevel { get; } Property Value int CorruptionPoints Corruption-point counter within the current tier; advances the tier when it reaches CorruptionPointsPerLevel. public int CorruptionPoints { get; } Property Value int Curse The unit’s curse value (used for status mechanics or debuffs). public float Curse { get; protected set; } Property Value float Description Descriptive text about the unit. public string Description { get; protected set; } Property Value string EntityProfile Designer-authored display metadata (portrait, class, level). Optional. public EntityProfile? EntityProfile { get; protected set; } Property Value EntityProfile Faction Faction this unit fights for. public Faction Faction { get; protected set; } Property Value Faction Hp The current health points of the unit. public float Hp { get; protected set; } Property Value float Intelligence The unit's intelligence stat, affecting magical power or effects. public float Intelligence { get; protected set; } Property Value float Inventory public IInventorySystem Inventory { get; protected set; } Property Value IInventorySystem IsAlive Indicates whether the unit is alive. public bool IsAlive { get; protected set; } Property Value bool IsControlled Tell if the unit is controlled or not public bool IsControlled { get; } Property Value bool Mana The current mana points of the unit. public float Mana { get; protected set; } Property Value float ManaMax The unit's available mana points for casting skills. public float ManaMax { get; protected set; } Property Value float MaxHp The maximum health points of the unit. public float MaxHp { get; protected set; } Property Value float PassiveSkills List of passive (always-on) skills applied to this unit. public List<ISkillSystem> PassiveSkills { get; protected set; } Property Value List<ISkillSystem> Personality The default AI personality type of the unit. public AIPersonality Personality { get; protected set; } Property Value AIPersonality PortraitTexture The portrait texture displayed for this unit in the UI. public Texture2D? PortraitTexture { get; protected set; } Property Value Texture2D PossibleMovesRange The maximum number of tiles the unit can move per turn. public int PossibleMovesRange { get; protected set; } Property Value int TotalAtk public float TotalAtk { get; } Property Value float TotalDef public float TotalDef { get; } Property Value float Type The type or archetype of the unit. public AovDataStructures.UnitType Type { get; protected set; } Property Value AovDataStructures.UnitType UnitName The name of the unit. public string UnitName { get; protected set; } Property Value string Methods AdjustAttack(float) Adjust the unit's base attack by delta (signed). Used by curse / buff effects that shift raw stats persistently. public virtual void AdjustAttack(float delta) Parameters delta float Signed delta. Negative reduces, positive increases. AdjustDefense(float) Adjust the unit's base defense by delta (signed). public virtual void AdjustDefense(float delta) Parameters delta float Signed delta. Negative reduces, positive increases. AdjustSpeed(float) Adjust the unit's base speed by delta (signed). public virtual void AdjustSpeed(float delta) Parameters delta float Signed delta. Negative reduces, positive increases. ApplyEffect(StatusEffect) Apply a legacy non-generic status effect to this unit. Stacks if already present and stackable; otherwise stores it and fires OnApply. public void ApplyEffect(StatusEffect statusEffect) Parameters statusEffect StatusEffect Legacy effect instance. AssignFaction(Faction) Backward-compatible alias for SetFaction(Faction). public void AssignFaction(Faction faction) Parameters faction Faction Faction to assign. BypassDamage(float) Applies damage that bypasses defense to the unit and updates HP. public virtual void BypassDamage(float damage) Parameters damage float The amount of damage to apply. CanMoveTo(int, int, int, IMapSystem) Checks if the unit can move to a given position. public virtual bool CanMoveTo(int x, int y, int z, IMapSystem map) Parameters x int The X coordinate. y int The Y coordinate (height). z int The Z coordinate. map IMapSystem The map to check against. Returns bool true if the position is walkable; otherwise false. Cleanup() Cleans up the unit instance. This method can be overridden in derived classes to implement specific cleanup logic. protected virtual void Cleanup() Remarks This method is called when the unit is removed from the scene tree. It provides a place for derived classes to implement any necessary cleanup logic, such as disconnecting signals or releasing resources. By default, it does nothing, but derived classes can override it to perform specific cleanup tasks. Remarks This method uses a Breadth-First Search (BFS) algorithm to evaluate all valid moves considering walkable tiles and vertical traversal (e.g. stairs, cliffs). GetReachableCellsForSkills(IMapSystem, ISkillSystem) Calculates all reachable cells for this unit based on the skill range and the unit position. public virtual List<(int, int, int)> GetReachableCellsForSkills(IMapSystem map, ISkillSystem skill) Parameters map IMapSystem The map to evaluate reachable cells on. skill ISkillSystem The selected skill Returns List<(int, int, int)> A list of reachable coordinates (x, y, z). Remarks This method uses a Breadth-First Search (BFS) algorithm to evaluate all valid cells considering vertical traversal (e.g. stairs, cliffs). HasEffect<T>() Checks whether this target currently has an active status effect of the specified type. public bool HasEffect<T>() where T : StatusEffect<IUnitSystem> Returns bool true if the effect is present on this target; otherwise, false. Type Parameters T The type of StatusEffect<TTarget> to check for. Heal(float) Restore HP by amount, clamped to MaxHp. It ensures that the unit instance is properly cleaned up and set to null. api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.html.title: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems | Ashes of Velsingrad Documentation
api/AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems.html.summary: Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.Systems Classes BattleInputSystem Central system responsible for handling player inputs during battle phases. CellInformation Represents the information for a single grid cell in the map, including its coordinates, ground type, walkability, and the unit occupying it. EffectTarget<TTarget> Base class for any entity that can receive and manage StatusEffect<TTarget>s. Provides default implementations for applying, removing, and querying effects. EquipmentSystem FactionExtensions Convenience helpers for reasoning about Faction relationships. InventorySystem ItemCatalog ItemSystem MapSystem Base class for all playable maps in the game. SkillSystem Base class for all skills available in the game. StatusEffectSystem Manages the lifecycle and application of StatusEffect<TTarget>s for all tracked targets in the game, including units and map cells. StatusEffect<TTarget> Base class for all status effects that can be applied to an IEffectTarget<TTarget>. UnitSystem Base class for all units in the tactical grid-based system. Handles stats, movement, effects, and Godot integration. Structs InventorySlot Interfaces IEffectTarget<TTarget> Represents any entity that can receive and manage StatusEffect<TTarget>s. IInteractable Defines a contract for objects that can be interacted with by the player. Implementing classes handle interaction logic, availability checks, and optional UI feedback such as prompts. IInteractor Represents an entity capable of initiating interactions with interactable objects. IInventorySlot IInventorySystem IItemSystem IMapSystem ISkillSystem IStatusEffect Empty interface to be able to contain any status effect as the actual StatusEffect<TTarget> abstract class cannot take object as the TTarget IStatusEffectBehavior Defines the behavior contract for status effects applied to an entity. IUnitSystem Contract for every combatant in the battle layer. Enums EquipSlot Faction Identifies which side a combatant fights for. ItemCategory TargetType

area lets map clicks through. Each button raises a C# event the rest of the HUD wires up. The "Pass" button additionally calls IUnitSystem.PassTurn directly so the turn loop unblocks even if the consumer didn't subscribe. The Cancel button is hidden until ShowCancel(bool) is invoked by GameManager when entering skill-targeting mode. Methods EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() SetInventoryUI(BattleInventoryUI, SkillSelector, Func<InventorySystem?>) Connect the action menu to the inventory panel and skill selector so "Use Item" can swap them in place. public void SetInventoryUI(BattleInventoryUI inventoryUI, SkillSelector skillSelector, Func<InventorySystem?> getActiveInventory) Parameters inventoryUI BattleInventoryUI skillSelector SkillSelector getActiveInventory Func<InventorySystem> ShowCancel(bool) Show or hide the Cancel button. Called by GameManager when the player enters or leaves skill-targeting mode. public void ShowCancel(bool show) Parameters show bool True to show the Cancel button. _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready() Events OnAttackPressed Player chose the basic-attack action. public event Action? OnAttackPressed Event Type Action OnCancelPressed Player cancelled skill targeting (Cancel button / Esc / right-click). public event Action? OnCancelPressed Event Type Action OnMovePressed Player chose the move action (returns to MoveUnit click context). public event Action? OnMovePressed Event Type Action OnPassPressed Player chose to pass the turn. public event Action? OnPassPressed Event Type Action OnSkillPressed Player asked to open / focus the skill submenu. public event Action? OnSkillPressed Event Type Action

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Spawned (or found) by GameManager at battle start. Children are created programmatically in _Ready() if a .tscn didn't pre-instantiate them. Each child widget self-positions; the HUD itself doesn't subscribe to events — it just exposes the children so GameManager can wire them. Fields HudLayer CanvasLayer index. Pinned high so the HUD always renders above the 3D viewport content and any other CanvasLayers in the scene. public const int HudLayer = 100 Field Value int Properties ActionMenu Action menu (Move / Attack / Skill / Pass / Cancel). public ActionMenu? ActionMenu { get; } Property Value ActionMenu ContextInfo Top-left context panel (movement budget vs queued skill details). public ContextInfoPanel? ContextInfo { get; } Property Value ContextInfoPanel EnemyRoster Top-right vertical roster of enemies. public EnemyRoster? EnemyRoster { get; } Property Value EnemyRoster InventoryPanel public BattleInventoryUI? InventoryPanel { get; } Property Value BattleInventoryUI Log Right-side scrolling log of warnings + events. public BattleLog? Log { get; } Property Value BattleLog PlayerStatus Bottom-left status panel for the active controlled unit. public PlayerStatusPanel? PlayerStatus { get; } Property Value PlayerStatusPanel SkillSelector Five-slot active-skill bar. public SkillSelector? SkillSelector { get; } Property Value SkillSelector TurnQueue Top-centre upcoming-turn-order strip. public TurnOrderQueue? TurnQueue { get; } Property Value TurnOrderQueue Methods Build() Build (or re-resolve) the child widget references, pin the layer's render order, and force every child widget's own layout to run synchronously. Idempotent — safe to call from GameManager.EnsureHud right after AddChild so the HUD is fully usable without waiting for _Ready to fire. public void Build() _ExitTree() Called when the node is about to leave the Godot.SceneTree (e.g. upon freeing, scene changing, or after calling Godot.Node.RemoveChild(Godot.Node) in a script). If the node has children, its Godot.Node._ExitTree() callback will be called last, after all its children have left the tree. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationExitTree notification in _Notification(int) and signal Godot.Node.TreeExiting. To get notified when the node has already left the active tree, connect to the Godot.Node.TreeExited. public override void _ExitTree() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Producers (GameManager, skills, status effects) call BattleNotifications.Post(message, severity) and the log appends a colour-coded line. Caps at MaxLines entries. Fields MaxLines Maximum number of log lines retained on screen. public const int MaxLines = 60 Field Value int Methods EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() _ExitTree() Called when the node is about to leave the Godot.SceneTree (e.g. upon freeing, scene changing, or after calling Godot.Node.RemoveChild(Godot.Node) in a script). If the node has children, its Godot.Node._ExitTree() callback will be called last, after all its children have left the tree. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationExitTree notification in _Notification(int) and signal Godot.Node.TreeExiting. To get notified when the node has already left the active tree, connect to the Godot.Node.TreeExited. public override void _ExitTree() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

reachable-tile budget and a one-line hint. Action phase (after a move): shows "move spent, pick an action". Skill targeting: shows the selected skill's full details + cancel hint. Driven directly by GameManager through ShowMovement(int, int, bool) and ShowSkill(ISkillSystem) — no event subscription, since the panel is a pure view of the input state machine. Methods Clear() Reset to a placeholder state when no battle is active. public void Clear() EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() ShowMovement(int, int, bool) Display the movement summary for the active turn. public void ShowMovement(int reachableCount, int rangeBudget, bool canMove) Parameters reachableCount int Number of reachable tiles shown on the map. rangeBudget int The unit's PossibleMovesRange. canMove bool Whether the unit can still move this turn. ShowSkill(ISkillSystem) Display details for a queued skill while the player picks a target. public void ShowSkill(ISkillSystem skill) Parameters skill ISkillSystem Skill currently being targeted. _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Shows two actions: Try Again reloads the current scene to restart the encounter, Forfeit raises an event the rest of the project will wire up later (return-to-menu, save the failure flag, etc.). Sits at GameOverLayer, above both the in-battle HUD (100) and the VictoryScreen (110), since these two screens are mutually exclusive but the higher layer guarantees nothing peeks through. Fields GameOverLayer CanvasLayer index above HUD and VictoryScreen. public const int GameOverLayer = 120 Field Value int Methods EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready() Events OnForfeitPressed Fired when the player presses Forfeit. Wired to nothing real yet — the project will hook this up to a "return to menu" or "fail-state save" handler later. public event Action? OnForfeitPressed Event Type Action OnTryAgainPressed Fired when the player presses Try Again — reload the current scene. public event Action? OnTryAgainPressed Event Type Action

api/AshesOfVelsingrad.UI.Hud.HudStyle.ht.summary: Class HudStyle Namespace AshesOfVelsingrad.UI.Hud Assembly AshesofVelsingrad.dll Shared visual constants and helpers for every HUD widget — colours, panel styles, bar styles, label / button theme overrides. public static class HudStyle Inheritance object HudStyle Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Remarks Keeps the HUD's look-and-feel in one file. A future iteration can replace this with a Godot Godot.Theme resource without changing widget code. Fields FontSizeBody Body font size (most labels, buttons). public const int FontSizeBody = 14 Field Value int FontSizeHeader Section-header font size (panel titles, unit name above HP). public const int FontSizeHeader = 16 Field Value int FontSizeMetaKey Meta key identifying the design-time font size of a styled control. public const string FontSizeMetaKey = "hud_base_font_size" Field Value string FontSizePropertyMetaKey Meta key identifying the theme override property used (e.g. "font_size", "normal_font_size"). public const string FontSizePropertyMetaKey = "hud_font_size_property" Field Value string FontSizeSmall Footnote font size (log lines, dim subtitles). public const int FontSizeSmall = 13 Field Value int FontSizeTitle Hero font size (Victory / GameOver title). public const int FontSizeTitle = 36 Field Value int Properties DimText Dimmed text colour (used for placeholders / labels). public static Color DimText { get; } Property Value Color EnemyColor Enemy chip colour for the turn queue. public static Color EnemyColor { get; } Property Value Color HpFill Healthy HP bar fill. public static Color HpFill { get; } Property Value Color ManaFill Mana bar fill. public static Color ManaFill { get; } Property Value Color PanelBackground Translucent dark background used by every HUD panel. public static Color PanelBackground { get; } Property Value Color PanelBorder Subtle border colour around panels. public static Color PanelBorder { get; } Property Value Color PlayerColor Player chip colour for the turn queue. public static Color PlayerColor { get; } Property Value Color TextColor Foreground text colour. public static Color TextColor { get; } Property Value Color Methods ApplyBarStyle(ProgressBar, Color) Build a HP/MP-style stylebox pair for a Godot.ProgressBar. public static void ApplyBarStyle(ProgressBar bar, Color fillColor) Parameters bar ProgressBar The progress bar to style. fillColor Color Colour for the filled portion. ApplyScaledFontSize(Control, string, int) Apply a UI-scaled font size override to control AND remember the design-time base so a later RefreshScaledFonts(Node) walk can re-apply it cleanly. public static void ApplyScaledFontSize(Control control, string property, int baseSize) Parameters control Control Control receiving the override. property string Theme override property name ("font_size" for Label/Button, "normal_font_size" for RichTextLabel). baseSize int Design-time font size. MakePanel(Control) Wrap content in a styled Godot.PanelContainer filling the parent. public static PanelContainer MakePanel(Control content) Parameters content Control Control to host inside the panel. Returns PanelContainer A panel containing content, with MouseFilter.Ignore so empty space lets clicks through to the map. MakePanelStyle() Build a translucent rounded panel Godot.StyleBoxFlat. public static StyleBoxFlat MakePanelStyle() Returns StyleBoxFlat A reusable stylebox. RefreshScaledFonts(Node) Walk root's descendants and re-apply font-size overrides for every control that was tagged via ApplyScaledFontSize(Control, string, int) or one of the StyleLabel/StyleButton helpers. Cheap — only touches Controls that opted in. public static void RefreshScaledFonts(Node root) Parameters root Node Subtree root (typically the BattleHud CanvasLayer). ScaledFontSize(int) Returns baseSize multiplied by the user's UI scale setting (GetUiScale()). Always returns at least 1 so Godot's font renderer never gets a zero/negative size. public static int ScaledFontSize(int baseSize) Parameters baseSize int Design-time font size — one of the FontSizeTitle/FontSizeHeader/ FontSizeBody/FontSizeSmall constants. Returns int Scaled, integer-rounded font size suitable for AddThemeFontSizeOverride(StringName, int). StyleButton(Button, int) Apply default text colour and a UI-scaled font size to a Godot.Button. public static void StyleButton(Button button, int baseSize = 14) Parameters button Button Button to style. baseSize int Design-time font size; defaults to FontSizeBody. StyleLabel(Label, int) Apply default text colour and a UI-scaled font size to a Godot.Label. public static void StyleLabel(Label label, int baseSize = 14) Parameters label Label Label to style. baseSize int Design-time font size; defaults to FontSizeBody.

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Bound by GameManager on every player turn start (and after any HP/MP-changing action). Light by design — the new branch doesn't yet have EntityProfile (portrait/level/class metadata), so the panel just shows the name and the bars. Add the portrait/level rendering back once a profile resource lands. Methods Bind(IUnitSystem?) Bind the panel to a unit. Pass null to reset. public void Bind(IUnitSystem? unit) Parameters unit IUnitSystem Unit to display, or null. EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() Refresh() Re-read the bound unit's stats and repaint. public void Refresh() _Process(double) Called on each idle frame, prior to rendering, and after physics ticks have been processed. delta is the time between frames in seconds. It is only called if processing is enabled for this Node, which is done automatically if this method is overridden, and can be toggled with SetProcess(bool). Processing happens in order of Godot.Node.ProcessPriority, lower priority values are called first. Nodes with the same priority are processed in tree order, or top to bottom as seen in the editor (also known as pre-order traversal). Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationProcess notification in _Notification(int). Note: This method is only called if the node is present in the scene tree (i.e. if it's not an orphan). Note: When the engine is struggling and the frame rate is lowered, delta will increase. When delta is increased, it's capped at a maximum of Godot.Engine.TimeScale * Godot.Engine.MaxPhysicsStepsPerFrame / Godot.Engine.PhysicsTicksPerSecond. As a result, accumulated delta may not represent real world time. Note: When --fixed-fps is enabled or the engine is running in Movie Maker mode (see Godot.MovieWriter), process delta will always be the same for every frame, regardless of how much time the frame took to render. Note: Frame delta may be post-processed by Godot.OS.DeltaSmoothing if this is enabled for the project. public override void _Process(double delta) Parameters delta double _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Reads from the active player unit's ActiveSkills list. Each slot's button is disabled when the skill is on cooldown or the caster has insufficient mana. Hover tooltips carry the full description / MP / CD / Range. The widget is purely presentational — it raises OnSkillSelected with the slot index and the resolved skill, and the consumer (GameManager) decides what to do (enter targeting, etc.). Fields SlotCount Number of slots, fixed at 5. public const int SlotCount = 5 Field Value int Methods Bind(IUnitSystem?) Bind the selector to the unit whose loadout to display. Refreshes immediately. Also hides the widget entirely on non-player turns — the player can only act on Player units, so showing AI-driven skills would be misleading. public void Bind(IUnitSystem? unit) Parameters unit IUnitSystem Unit to track, or null to clear. EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() Refresh() Re-read the bound unit's skills and refresh slot text + disabled state. public void Refresh() _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready() Events OnSkillSelected Fired when a slot is clicked. Carries the slot index (0-4) and the skill. public event Action<int, ISkillSystem>? OnSkillSelected Event Type Action<int, ISkillSystem>

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Cyan border = Player, green = Ally, red = Enemy. The first chip is highlighted as the currently acting unit. When a unit's EntityProfile has a portrait, it's shown inside the chip; otherwise a coloured square fallback fills the space. UpdateOrder(IReadOnlyList<IUnitSystem>) is called by GameManager after every turn transition with the upcoming units (typically from TurnManager.GetUpcomingUnits). Methods EnsureBuilt() Idempotent build — safe to call before _Ready fires. public void EnsureBuilt() UpdateOrder(IReadOnlyList<IUnitSystem>) Replace the current chip strip with the given upcoming units. public void UpdateOrder(IReadOnlyList<IUnitSystem> upcomingUnits) Parameters upcomingUnits IReadOnlyList<IUnitSystem> Units to show, in activation order. Index 0 is currently acting. _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()

GodotObject.PropertyListChanged object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) Remarks Bind once with Bind(IUnitSystem?) and call Refresh() from the parent widget whenever an event source (a turn change, a damage tick, etc.) might have altered the unit's state. Designed to be re-used by EnemyRoster and other widgets that aggregate per-unit summaries. Methods Bind(IUnitSystem?) Bind the bar to a unit and immediately refresh. public void Bind(IUnitSystem? unit) Parameters unit IUnitSystem Unit to track. Pass null to clear. Refresh() Re-read the bound unit's HP/MP and update the visuals. public void Refresh() _Process(double) Called on each idle frame, prior to rendering, and after physics ticks have been processed. delta is the time between frames in seconds. It is only called if processing is enabled for this Node, which is done automatically if this method is overridden, and can be toggled with SetProcess(bool). Processing happens in order of Godot.Node.ProcessPriority, lower priority values are called first. Nodes with the same priority are processed in tree order, or top to bottom as seen in the editor (also known as pre-order traversal). Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationProcess notification in _Notification(int). Note: This method is only called if the node is present in the scene tree (i.e. if it's not an orphan). Note: When the engine is struggling and the frame rate is lowered, delta will increase. When delta is increased, it's capped at a maximum of Godot.Engine.TimeScale * Godot.Engine.MaxPhysicsStepsPerFrame / Godot.Engine.PhysicsTicksPerSecond. As a result, accumulated delta may not represent real world time. Note: When --fixed-fps is enabled or the engine is running in Movie Maker mode (see Godot.MovieWriter), process delta will always be the same for every frame, regardless of how much time the frame took to render. Note: Frame delta may be post-processed by Godot.OS.DeltaSmoothing if this is enabled for the project. public override void _Process(double delta) Parameters delta double _Ready() Called when the node is "ready", i.e. when both the node and its children have entered the scene tree. If the node has children, their Godot.Node._Ready() callbacks get triggered first, and the parent node will receive the ready notification afterwards. Corresponds to the Godot.Node.NotificationReady notification in _Notification(int). See also the @onready annotation for variables. Usually used for initialization. For even earlier initialization, Godot.GodotObject.GodotObject() may be used. See also Godot.Node._EnterTree(). Note: This method may be called only once for each node. After removing a node from the scene tree and adding it again, Godot.Node._Ready() will not be called a second time. This can be bypassed by requesting another call with Godot.Node.RequestReady(), which may be called anywhere before adding the node again. public override void _Ready()
